tasks.title: Fix run-loop stop signal handling (sequential + parallel)
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs:219-233 only checks stop signal on successful task completion; failures skip stop handling.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:263-282 logs stop signal but never clears it.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:446-454 does not exit when stop signal is set and no workers are in flight (next task exists => loop continues).
crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/stop.rs:7-8 notes signal cleanup is handled by the run loop after detection.
plan: In sequential run_loop, check for stop signal after any task attempt (success or failure), clear it, and exit cleanly.
In parallel run_loop, if stop signal is set and there are no in-flight workers, break the loop and clear the signal; avoid the idle spin when tasks remain but stop is requested.
Add regression coverage for stop-signal behavior in both sequential and parallel paths (including failure path and idle stop), and update docs if behavior changes.

tasks.title: Honor git_commit_push_enabled overrides in parallel workers
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/worker.rs:139-150 unconditionally adds --git-commit-push-on to worker args.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/args.rs:18-91 does not map git_commit_push_enabled overrides into CLI args, so --git-commit-push-off is ignored in parallel mode.
plan: Add git_commit_push_enabled mapping to build_override_args and remove/conditionalize the hardcoded --git-commit-push-on in worker command construction.
Decide whether auto_pr/auto_merge should force commit/push and document the behavior explicitly if it remains required.
Add tests for parallel worker arg construction with git_commit_push_on/off overrides and update CLI docs to reflect the final behavior.

tasks.title: Align docs with parallel.workers default behavior
evidence: docs/configuration.md:220-222 claims parallel is disabled unless CLI --parallel is used.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs:90-94 enables parallel whenever config.parallel.workers is set (even without --parallel).
plan: Decide whether parallel.workers should implicitly enable parallel runs without --parallel.
If code is correct, update docs/CLI help to state that setting parallel.workers enables parallel by default; otherwise, change run_loop to require --parallel and add a test for the intended behavior.
Verify help text and configuration docs are consistent across docs/configuration.md and docs/cli.md.

tasks.title: Make parallel loop avoid queue.json merge conflicts without losing task state
evidence: CLI: `ralph run loop --parallel <N>` enables parallel mode (clap wiring in `crates/ralph/src/cli/run.rs`, dispatch in `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs`).
Parallel mode clones workspaces and copies `.ralph/queue.json` and `.ralph/done.json` into each workspace (`sync_ralph_state` in `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/sync.rs`).

Parallel workers run `ralph run one --parallel-worker` in the workspace; `mark_task_doing` writes `.ralph/queue.json` via `queue::save_queue` (`crates/ralph/src/commands/run/context.rs`, `crates/ralph/src/queue.rs`).

Merge runner attempts to merge PRs and can invoke AI conflict resolution (`crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs`), so worker-branch edits to `.ralph/queue.json`/`.ralph/done.json` can repeatedly surface as Git merge conflicts.

Parallel state persists in `.ralph/cache/parallel/state.json` (`crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/state.rs`) and the supervisor holds a queue lock for selection, but Git-level conflicts still occur when multiple branches modify the same queue/done files.
plan: Reproduce on a clean branch: run `ralph run loop --parallel 2 --max-tasks 2`, then capture which PR/merge step produces conflicts on `.ralph/queue.json` and/or `.ralph/done.json` (include `git status` + conflict file list).

Inventory all queue/done mutation sites that can run inside a parallel worker (e.g., `mark_task_doing`, task completion transitions, queue save/archive helpers) and map them to the parallel workflow (workspace sync → worker run → PR creation → merge).

Define the desired parallel invariant: only the coordinator (base branch) mutates `.ralph/queue.json`/`.ralph/done.json`; workers emit a minimal, deterministic per-task "delta" (status transitions + timestamps + any task field updates) for the coordinator to apply under the queue lock.

Implement coordinator-applied deltas in `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs` (or adjacent module) and ensure worker branches do not include tracked changes to `.ralph/queue.json`/`.ralph/done.json` (adjust `sync_ralph_state`/worker setup accordingly).

Add tests that simulate multiple workers completing in close succession and assert: no queue/done diffs in worker branches, no dropped tasks, no queue wipes, completed tasks reliably move to done, and reruns are idempotent.

Update `docs/cli.md` and `ralph run loop --help` to document parallel queue semantics (explicitly: coordinator-managed queue/done, how completion is applied, and any behavior changes vs non-parallel).
Run `make ci` and `ralph queue validate`, then do a manual merge of two worker branches to confirm `.ralph/queue.json`/`.ralph/done.json` are conflict-free.
notes: `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/loop/` no longer exists; run loop dispatch (including parallel `--parallel`) lives in `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs` and clap wiring is in `crates/ralph/src/cli/run.rs`.

Parallel state is persisted at `.ralph/cache/parallel/state.json`; the current parallel workflow copies queue/done into each workspace and worker runs write to queue, which aligns with the reported persistent merge conflicts.
Force-add parallel worker completion signals so ignored cache paths don't drop status/notes.
Added git helper for forced staging and exercised it in supervision tests.
CI: make ci; ralph queue validate.
tasks.request: When using 'ralph run loop --parallel 2' there are ALWAYS merge conflicts due to the queue.json changes. We need to figure out a way to handle this to avoid merge conflicts. We need to make sure we don't wipe out the queue and we properly mark tasks complete and move the tasks to the done.json file too. We need to be able to reliably merge and mimic the currently reliable workflow of 'ralph run loop' when it runs without parallel workers.

tasks.title: Fix parallel state.json to auto-correct and auto-populate git branch
evidence: User request: parallel run previously wrote `.ralph/cache/parallel/state.json` with base_branch='02022026'; later running on base_branch='main' fails unless the user manually clears the state file.

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs bails early when `existing.base_branch != current_branch` with: "Parallel state base branch '{}' does not match current branch '{}'" (no auto-correction path).
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs checks base_branch mismatch before pruning stale `tasks_in_flight`, so a stale state file can block cleanup/recovery.

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/state.rs hardcodes the state path to `.ralph/cache/parallel/state.json` and uses `serde(deny_unknown_fields)` + JSONC parsing, so legacy/unknown fields will fail deserialization.
crates/ralph/src/git/branch.rs resolves current branch via `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` and errors on detached HEAD, so parallel runs require a named branch.
Current repo state: `.ralph/cache/parallel/state.json` exists and currently has base_branch='main' with empty `tasks_in_flight`/`prs`, which is a safe shape for an auto-heal rewrite on mismatch.
plan: Read `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs` state bootstrap flow (load/save, base_branch validation, stale task pruning) and map the exact failure paths.

Define conservative auto-heal rules for base_branch mismatch (e.g., after pruning stale tasks and reconciling PR records, allow rewrite only when there are no in-flight tasks and no blocking/open PR records).

Implement base_branch auto-correction in the parallel startup path: if mismatch and state is safe-to-reset, rewrite state.base_branch to the current branch and persist; otherwise fail with an actionable error that references the state file path and recovery options.
Add unit tests that cover: mismatch + empty state auto-heals; mismatch + active tasks/prs stays a hard error; mismatch + stale PID/workspace is pruned then auto-heals.
Update `docs/cli.md` and `docs/workflow.md` to document the new behavior and the expected recovery workflow for parallel state files.
Run `make ci`.
notes: The current mismatch check runs before stale `tasks_in_flight` pruning; moving or augmenting this logic is likely required so crash-recovery can self-heal instead of hard-failing.
Auto-correction should avoid rewriting state when it could belong to an active parallel run (in-flight tasks or open PRs).
Guard missing base_branch by requiring safe state before auto-populating or retargeting.
Added tests for missing base_branch auto-heal and unsafe in-flight cases; kept mismatch coverage.
make ci passes.
tasks.request: Fix the state.json file to auto-correct and auto-populate and set the git branch properly. User previously ran a parallel run on branch '02022026' but has since changed to 'main' and this fails unless user manually clears .ralph/cache/parallel/state.json.

tasks.title: Prevent parallel loop from re-running tasks when no PR record exists
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/worker.rs:71-90 excludes in-flight tasks and only PRs with lifecycle==open && merged==false; tasks with no PR record are not excluded and can be selected again.

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:326-417 removes finished tasks from tasks_in_flight regardless of whether a PR record was created; PR records are only upserted inside the settings.auto_pr branches and PR creation failures only log warnings.

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:95-108 automatically disables auto_pr/auto_merge/draft_on_failure when git commit/push is disabled, making no-PR parallel runs possible even if config defaults enable PR automation.

docs/cli.md:581-591 states parallel workers do not modify .ralph/queue.json/.ralph/done.json and completion signals are applied only after a PR merge; if PR automation is skipped, the coordinator queue state stays unchanged and the same todo task remains runnable.
docs/configuration.md:216-240 documents that parallel.auto_pr can be set false (and is auto-disabled when commit/push is disabled), which currently allows repeated selection in parallel mode.

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/state.rs:11-14 and 24-37 define .ralph/cache/parallel/state.json with tasks_in_flight and prs only; there is no durable marker for finished tasks that have no PR record.
plan: Reproduce: run `ralph run loop --parallel 2 --max-tasks 2` with `parallel.auto_pr=false` (or `--git-commit-push-off`) and confirm the same task ID is selected again after completion.

Decide and document the intended policy for parallel runs when PR automation is disabled or PR creation fails (recommended: treat finished tasks as blocked/pending PR and prevent re-selection until user action).

Implement the policy: either hard-error when auto_pr is effectively disabled in parallel mode, or persist a durable needs_pr/completed_without_pr marker in .ralph/cache/parallel/state.json and include it in the exclusion set during selection.
Add tests for auto_pr=false, git-commit-push-off, and PR creation failure; assert tasks are not re-selected indefinitely and the state persists across restarts.
Update docs/configuration.md and docs/cli.md to describe the new behavior/constraints and any recovery steps for clearing blocked state.
notes: Today, selection can loop forever when a task finishes but no PR record exists (auto_pr disabled or gh PR creation failure), because queue/done remain unchanged and only open PRs are excluded.

Parallel already reconciles PR records on startup, but reconciliation does not update queue/done; consider whether merged PRs should remain blocking until the coordinator observes and applies completion updates.
Add finished-without-PR blockers to prevent parallel re-runs without PRs
Clarify recovery guidance for no-PR blockers in docs and logs
make ci

tasks.title: Ensure workspace cleanup when parallel worker spawn fails
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:267-283 creates/syncs a workspace then calls spawn_worker(..)? before guard.register_workspace(..); if spawn_worker fails, the function returns early and the workspace directory is left on disk.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:298-299 registers the workspace only after a successful spawn_worker, so failure paths never reach workspace registration.

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/cleanup_guard.rs:188-199 removes only workspaces tracked in ParallelCleanupGuard.workspaces (registered via register_workspace); it does not remove untracked workspace directories.
plan: In crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs, register the workspace with ParallelCleanupGuard immediately after git::create_workspace_at (and sync_ralph_state), before calling spawn_worker.
Confirm workspace cleanup remains safe and idempotent when a workspace is pre-registered (manual removals should continue to be no-ops due to path.exists checks).

Add regression coverage that forces a spawn_worker failure and asserts the workspace directory is removed via guard drop/cleanup (likely by injecting a failing spawner or factoring the spawn step into a testable helper).
Run cargo test -p ralph and make ci to verify parallel cleanup behavior and prevent regressions.
notes: Scope updated: removed non-existent crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/tests.rs; current unit tests for parallel cleanup live in crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/cleanup_guard.rs under #[cfg(test)].
Current code still registers workspaces after spawn_worker succeeds, so a spawn_worker error can leak a workspace directory until manually cleaned.
Register workspaces before sync/spawn so cleanup handles early failures.
Added regression tests for sync and spawn failure cleanup paths.
CI: make ci

tasks.title: Stop defaulting missing parallel completion signals to Done
evidence: Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:448-458 (ensure_completion_signal_in_workspace writes a completion signal with status TaskStatus::Done when missing)

Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:383-385 (apply_completion_and_collect_bytes calls ensure_completion_signal_in_workspace then apply_phase3_completion_signal, which can finalize the task)
Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/phase3.rs:298-347 (apply_phase3_completion_signal completes the task from the completion signal, then removes the signal)
Path: crates/ralph/src/completions.rs:85-93 (completion signals only allow terminal statuses done/rejected; there is no "unknown" status)
Test: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:970-1037 covers the happy-path (signal present); missing-signal behavior is currently untested
Repro: remove .ralph/cache/completions/<TASK_ID>.json on the base branch, then run parallel merge; merge runner synthesizes a Done completion signal and finalizes the task
plan: Change ensure_completion_signal_in_workspace to return an error when the completion signal is missing (do not synthesize TaskStatus::Done).
Update merge runner flow to surface the error and avoid mutating queue/done when the completion signal is missing.
Add a unit test in merge_runner.rs covering missing completion signal: expect an error and verify no queue/done mutations are applied.
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph`.
notes: Current merge runner behavior can incorrectly mark tasks Done during parallel merges when no completion signal exists; erroring is safer than guessing.
Completion signals only support done/rejected; representing an "unknown" status would be a larger contract change, so missing signals should be treated as a hard error here.
Stop treating missing parallel completion signals as Done; now errors if absent in base-sync.
Updated merge-runner tests and CLI/docs to reflect missing-signal error path.
CI: make ci

tasks.title: Validate queue/done bytes before applying merge results
evidence: Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs:890-925 (apply_merge_queue_sync persists queue/done/productivity bytes directly; bypasses queue parsing + semantic validation)

Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:423-452 (apply_completion_and_collect_bytes reads queue/done bytes from the base-sync workspace and returns them without validating as QueueFile JSONC + semantics)
Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:270-342 (validate_queue_done_in_workspace exists but is only invoked after AI conflict resolution, not for clean merges)
Risk/Repro: if a merged PR introduces unparseable or semantically invalid queue/done contents, the parallel merge flow can write those bytes into the local repo, breaking subsequent queue operations
plan: In apply_merge_queue_sync, parse queue_bytes and optional done_bytes into QueueFile values (use the same JSONC parsing rules as queue::load_queue) and validate them via queue::validate_queue/validate_queue_set before touching disk.
Serialize validated QueueFile values and atomically write via queue::save_queue (and remove done_path when done_bytes is None); avoid writing raw bytes from MergeResult.
On any parse/validation error, return an error and leave local queue/done/productivity untouched; include task_id and the failing context in the error chain.
Add unit tests for apply_merge_queue_sync: rejects invalid queue bytes, rejects invalid done bytes, accepts missing done bytes, and preserves existing files on failure.
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph`.
notes: merge_runner validates queue/done in the merge workspace only after AI conflict resolution, but the clean-merge path still produces bytes that are applied locally without any parse/validation gate.
queue parsing supports JSONC (comments + trailing commas) and semantic validation helpers exist, but apply_merge_queue_sync currently bypasses them entirely.
Validate merge queue/done bytes with JSONC parsing + semantic checks before write
Persist normalized queue/done via save_queue; skip writes on validation failure
Added apply_merge_queue_sync tests for parse/UTF-8/semantic failures and JSONC success
make ci

tasks.title: Reject PR heads that do not match the parallel branch prefix
evidence: Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:119-126 (task_id derived from PR head + configured branch_prefix)
Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:605-612 (task_id_from_branch strips prefix but falls back to returning the raw head when prefix mismatches)
Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:230 (workspace_root.join(task_id) treats task_id as a path segment; a task_id with '/' creates nested paths)
Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs:634-637 (unit test asserts prefix mismatch returns 'feature/RQ-0002', codifying the problematic behavior)

Path: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/state.rs:205-225 (ParallelPrRecord::pr_info prefers stored head; if branch_prefix changes, stored head may no longer match and merge runner currently derives an invalid task_id)
plan: Introduce a shared helper to parse task_id from PR head that requires prefix match, strips it, and validates the remaining task ID against resolved id_prefix/id_width (and rejects '/' or '..').
Update merge_runner to use the helper; on invalid/mismatched heads, log a warning with PR number/head and skip merging that PR without terminating the merge thread.
Persist a human-readable blocker/warning in the parallel state file so resume runs surface the mismatch and operators can fix config/branches intentionally.
Update tests: replace the current fallback behavior test with mismatch-is-error, and add coverage for invalid IDs (whitespace, wrong width/prefix, path separators).
Verify via `cargo test -p ralph`, then run `make ci`.
notes: merge_runner.rs module docs assume PR heads use the configured prefix, but task_id_from_branch currently accepts mismatches by returning the raw head.
Because task_id is used in filesystem paths (workspace roots and completion signal locations), allowing arbitrary head strings risks nested paths and broken queue/done lookups.
Validate parallel PR heads against branch_prefix and carry trusted task_id into merge runner.
Persist merge_blocker in parallel state and skip blocked PRs.
Added validation/blocker tests and documented branch_prefix head requirements.

evidence: code: crates/ralph/tests/parallel_gh_preflight_test.rs:104 uses .ralph/cache/parallel_state.json (WRONG)

tasks.title: Make webhook delivery non-blocking (async worker + bounded queue) to avoid blocking task transitions

Implement backpressure policy (`drop_oldest|drop_new|block_with_timeout`) with conservative defaults to protect interactive UX.
Keep current retry semantics but move sleeps/retries into the worker; ensure secrets remain redacted in logs.

Document delivery semantics (best-effort, bounded queue, failure logging) in `docs/configuration.md`.

notes: Implemented async webhook worker with bounded crossbeam-channel queue

Fixed capacity bounds validation to prevent rendezvous channel issues
Added 13 unit tests and 8 integration tests covering non-blocking behavior, retries, signatures, event filtering, backpressure policies
Updated documentation in docs/configuration.md with delivery semantics
CI passes: all tests pass, schema regenerated, clippy clean

tasks.title: TUI foundation: component architecture, focus management, and layout primitives
evidence: User request: implement core OpenTUI (TS + Bun) features in our Rust + Ratatui framework; details are in `/tmp/opentui-rust-features.md`.
`.ralph/README.md` documents that Ralph has a Ratatui-based TUI under `crates/ralph/src/tui/`.
`/tmp/opentui-rust-features.md` recommends a component-based architecture and calls out focus management and flexbox-like layout as core building blocks.
Current TUI already has `tui-textarea = 0.7` and `tui-scrollview = 0.5` in Cargo.toml, providing foundation for rich input and scrollable containers.
Current TUI has basic focus management via `FocusedPanel` enum in `app_panel.rs` with Tab/Shift-Tab navigation between list/details panels.
TUI code is organized into modular components: `app_*.rs` files for state management, `events/` for input handling, `render/` for drawing.
TUI has comprehensive event handling in `events/` with modes (normal, editing, command palette, etc.) and rendering in `render/` with panels and overlays.
Existing tests are in `crates/ralph/src/tui/tests/` and `crates/ralph/src/tui/events/tests/` demonstrating established testing patterns.

Audit current TUI event handling in `crates/ralph/src/tui/events/` and rendering in `crates/ralph/src/tui/render/` to identify the smallest vertical slice to migrate first.
Introduce a small internal component model (e.g., a `Component` trait + message routing) with private defaults (prefer `pub(crate)` over `pub`) in `crates/ralph/src/tui/components.rs`.
Extend existing focus management in `app_panel.rs` to support deterministic focus order, per-component focused state that influences rendering and input routing, and focus wraparound.

Add shared layout helpers in `crates/ralph/src/tui/layout.rs` that express common OpenTUI flex patterns using Ratatui `Layout` constraints; explicitly document the decision and when (if ever) to adopt `taffy` from `/tmp/opentui-rust-features.md`.

Keep this refactor incremental: one migrated screen first, then expand in follow-up tasks.

Dependencies `tui-textarea` and `tui-scrollview` are already available - leverage these for rich input and scrollable containers.
The TUI has well-established patterns: event handlers in `events/`, renderers in `render/`, and state in `app_*.rs` modules - follow these conventions.
Existing tests demonstrate patterns for testing TUI behavior - use them as reference for new tests.

tasks.title: Avoid panic when resuming timed-out sessions (remove unwrap in run loop)
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs:151-167: Timeout branch re-loads session and calls `.unwrap()` on Option (`session::load_session(&cache_dir)?.unwrap()`), which can panic if session.json disappears between the first load and the re-load.

crates/ralph/src/session/mod.rs:84-93 and :96-137: `SessionValidationResult::Timeout { hours }` does not carry `SessionState`; `validate_session` returns Timeout without session (crates/ralph/src/session/mod.rs:120-134), forcing callers to re-load if they need session details.
crates/ralph/src/session/mod.rs:140-151: `check_session` loads the session but returns only `SessionValidationResult`, so `run` cannot reuse the already-loaded `SessionState` on Timeout.
crates/ralph/src/session/mod.rs:500-531: unit test matches `SessionValidationResult::Timeout { hours }`; changing the enum shape will require updating this test (and any future match sites).
plan: Change `SessionValidationResult::Timeout` to carry the `SessionState` (e.g., `Timeout { hours, session }`) so `run` can prompt without re-loading session.json.
Update `validate_session` to return the new Timeout variant (including cloning the session, consistent with the existing `Valid(SessionState)` behavior).
Update `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs` to use the `session` from the Timeout variant and delete the `load_session(...).unwrap()` re-load path entirely.

Update unit tests in `crates/ralph/src/session/mod.rs` to match the new Timeout variant shape and add a regression test that exercises `check_session` timeout behavior without requiring a second disk load (no unwrap/panic path).
Verify via `cargo test -p ralph session` and `make ci`.
notes: Eliminated potential panic by removing session re-load + Option unwrap on timeout in run loop
Session timeout validation now returns Timeout { hours, session } so callers can reuse already-loaded SessionState
Updated session timeout tests and added regression test covering check_session timeout includes embedded SessionState
Verified: cargo test -p ralph session; make ci

tasks.title: Fix queue validation failure when custom_fields contains numeric values
evidence: Task contract defines `custom_fields: HashMap<String, String>` (crates/ralph/src/contracts/task.rs), so non-string JSON values will fail queue parsing during `jsonc::parse_jsonc::<QueueFile>` before semantic validation runs (crates/ralph/src/jsonc.rs, crates/ralph/src/queue.rs).

Queue JSON repair (`queue::load_queue_with_repair`) addresses syntax issues (quotes/trailing commas) but cannot fix type mismatches like numeric `custom_fields` because deserialization still requires strings (crates/ralph/src/queue.rs).
Queue schema defines `custom_fields` as an object with `additionalProperties.type = string` (schemas/queue.schema.json).
Docs define `custom_fields` as a map of strings (docs/queue-and-tasks.md) and CLI/TUI editing formats as `key=value` string pairs (docs/cli.md, docs/tui-task-management.md).

Task Builder and Task Updater prompts do not currently state that `custom_fields` values must be quoted JSON strings (crates/ralph/assets/prompts/task_builder.md, crates/ralph/assets/prompts/task_updater.md).

`task update` creates a queue backup and validates after the runner finishes, but if post-update parsing/validation fails it returns an error and leaves the invalid queue file on disk (backup path is only logged) (crates/ralph/src/commands/task/update.rs).

Run loop aborts after 50 consecutive failures with a generic systemic-issue message (crates/ralph/src/constants.rs, crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs), which can mask a repeated queue parse/validate failure if the queue is left invalid.
plan: Reproduce with a regression test/fixture: create a queue entry containing `"custom_fields": {"guide_line_count": 1411}` and assert `ralph queue validate` (or `queue::load_queue`) fails today due to a deserialization type mismatch (not semantic validation).

Decide contract (recommended): accept JSON scalars for `custom_fields` on read (string/number/bool) by coercing to strings, while keeping `Task.custom_fields` as `HashMap<String, String>` for CLI/TUI compatibility; reject arrays/objects/null with a clear error.

Implement custom deserialization for `Task.custom_fields` to coerce numbers/bools to strings and emit a descriptive error for unsupported value types; add unit tests covering string/number/bool success and array/object/null failure cases.

Update schema + docs to match the chosen contract: either broaden `schemas/queue.schema.json` (and docs) to allow numeric/bool values, or explicitly document that numeric/bool values are accepted but coerced to strings on load and will be serialized as strings on write.

Update `crates/ralph/assets/prompts/task_builder.md` and `crates/ralph/assets/prompts/task_updater.md` with an explicit rule + example showing `custom_fields` values as quoted JSON strings (e.g., `{"guide_line_count": "1411"}`) to reduce invalid agent writes.

Add a safety net to `task update`: if post-run queue parse/validation fails, automatically restore the pre-run backup so the queue is never left in an invalid state (prevents run loop from repeatedly failing and hitting the 50-failure abort).
Run `make ci`.
notes: This failure is a JSON decoding/contract mismatch (non-string value into `HashMap<String, String>`), not a semantic validator error; either accept scalar values during deserialization (coerce to strings) or ensure task update always rolls back to a valid queue on failure.
Queue JSON repair currently focuses on syntax repairs; it does not address type mismatches like numeric `custom_fields` values.
Accept custom_fields scalar values (string/number/bool) by coercing them to strings on load; reject null/arrays/objects with clear errors
Harden task update: after runner modifies queue, auto-restore from backup if parse/validation/save fails so the run loop can’t get stuck on a corrupted queue
Update schema/docs/prompts to reflect coercion behavior and recommend quoting custom_fields values
Add regression tests for coercion + failure modes and queue normalization
tasks.request: If a task in queue.json has values for a key set as numbers, e.g. "custom_fields.guide_line_count": 1411, the queue does not pass validation and breaks the ralph run loop after the task update phase. Either allow numbers or update the task build prompt to enforce strings instead of numbers in the JSON. Error: Run loop aborted after 50 consecutive task failures. This usually indicates a systemic issue (e.g., repo dirty, runner misconfiguration, or interrupt flag stuck). Check logs above for root cause.

tasks.title: Make LFS health checks fail loud (stop swallowing git/lfs errors)
evidence: crates/ralph/src/git/lfs.rs:539-542: rustdoc for `check_lfs_health` says it returns `Err(anyhow::Error)` if validation fails.
crates/ralph/src/git/lfs.rs:565-572: `check_lfs_health` discards errors via `.ok()` / `unwrap_or_default()` (e.g. `check_lfs_status(repo_root).ok()`, `list_lfs_files(repo_root).unwrap_or_default()`).
crates/ralph/src/git/lfs.rs:168-186: `LfsHealthReport::is_healthy` ignores missing `filter_status` / `status_summary` (`Option` fields), so suppressed errors can still yield a "healthy" report.
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/supervision/git_ops.rs:85-101: `warn_if_modified_lfs` logs and returns `Ok(())` when `has_lfs`/`check_lfs_health` return `Err` (even when `strict` is true).
plan: Define failure semantics for `check_lfs_health`: treat unexpected `git lfs status` / `git lfs ls-files` failures as `Err`, but keep the existing "LFS not installed / not initialized" stderr carve-outs as `Ok` defaults.

In `crates/ralph/src/git/lfs.rs`, replace `.ok()` / `unwrap_or_default()` with explicit error handling: propagate errors with `?` (or capture them into the report if we choose to keep `Ok(report)` in all cases).

In `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/supervision/git_ops.rs`, make `warn_if_modified_lfs(..., strict=true)` fail loud: propagate `has_lfs` / `check_lfs_health` errors instead of logging and returning `Ok(())`.

Add unit tests for the new contract: create a temp dir with a `.gitattributes` containing `filter=lfs` but no `.git/`, and assert (a) `check_lfs_health` returns `Err` and (b) `warn_if_modified_lfs(..., strict=true)` returns `Err` (and `strict=false` returns `Ok(())`).
Update rustdoc for `check_lfs_health` (and examples) to reflect the final error/return behavior.
Verify with `make test` and `make ci`.
notes: Repo state as of 2026-02-05: `check_lfs_health` suppresses `git lfs` command failures, and the supervision callsite (`warn_if_modified_lfs`) also suppresses `Err` paths by logging and continuing.
Propagate unexpected git/LFS failures from check_lfs_health (no more .ok()/unwrap_or_default swallowing)
warn_if_modified_lfs(strict=true) now fails loud on detection/health/status/list errors; strict=false preserves warn-and-continue
validate_lfs_filters now treats fatal git config failures as errors while missing keys remain a misconfig signal
Added regression tests for strict/non-strict error propagation using a repo with corrupted .git/config
Stabilized with_scope logging unit tests to ignore unrelated parallel-test logs

tasks.title: Fix productivity streak/velocity date math (remove 30-day-month approximation)
evidence: crates/ralph/src/productivity.rs:295-326: get_previous_date uses approximate month lengths (Feb=28) and hardcoded parse fallbacks (`unwrap_or(2026)`, `unwrap_or(1)`); explicitly notes it doesn't account for leap years.
crates/ralph/src/productivity.rs:393-419: get_date_offset converts to days using `year*365 + month*30 + day` and reconstructs `year/month/day` using `/365` and `/30` (incorrect calendar math).
crates/ralph/src/productivity.rs:674-692: test_velocity_calculation computes "yesterday" by decrementing the day-of-month only, so it doesn't exercise month/year boundary behavior.
User impact: streak and velocity calculations can be wrong around month/year boundaries.
plan: Replace `get_previous_date` and `get_date_offset` with `time` crate date arithmetic (parse YYYY-MM-DD into `time::Date` or parse `YYYY-MM-DDT00:00:00Z` into `time::OffsetDateTime`, then add/subtract `time::Duration::days`).

Remove hardcoded fallback year/month/day defaults; make invalid date handling explicit (e.g., return `Option<String>`/`Result<String>` and have callers treat invalid inputs as no streak advance / no velocity contribution).
Add unit tests for boundaries: 2024-03-01 previous day == 2024-02-29; 2026-03-01 previous day == 2026-02-28; 2026-01-01 previous day == 2025-12-31; offsets across month/year boundaries.
Update `test_velocity_calculation` to use fixed dates (not "today" and naive day decrement) so it reliably covers boundary behavior.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.
notes: As of 2026-02-06, the module still uses ad-hoc calendar logic for streak/velocity; the existing tests don't cover leap years or month/year rollovers.
Replaced streak/velocity date math with time::Date checked day arithmetic (no 30-day-month approximation).
Removed hardcoded parse fallbacks; invalid date keys now act as safe no-ops for streak/velocity.
Added regression tests for leap day + month/year boundaries and deterministic velocity window.
Kept calculate_velocity public signature stable; test-only helper uses fixed today key.

tasks.title: Prevent committing unredacted debug logs (.ralph/logs/)
evidence: File: `crates/ralph/src/debuglog.rs` creates `.ralph/logs/debug.log` and documents that it captures raw, unredacted supervisor and runner output.
File: `SECURITY.md` warns `--debug` writes raw (unredacted) runner output to `.ralph/logs/debug.log` and recommends adding `.ralph/logs/` to `.gitignore`.
File: `docs/workflow.md` repeats the recommendation to gitignore `.ralph/logs/` because debug logs capture unredacted output.
File: `docs/cli.md` and `docs/error-handling.md` both call out that debug logs capture raw output and should be gitignored.
File: `.gitignore` currently ignores `.ralph/lock` and many `.ralph/cache/*` entries but does not ignore `.ralph/logs/`.
File: `Makefile` removes `.ralph/logs/` under `clean`, implying debug logs are expected to exist locally and should not be committed.
File: `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/sync.rs` explicitly treats `.ralph/logs/` as an exclude, reinforcing that logs are sensitive/ephemeral.

Command: `git check-ignore -n -v .ralph/logs/debug.log` shows `.ralph/logs` is ignored on this machine via a user global excludesfile (`/Users/mitchfultz/dotfiles/git_global/.gitignore_global`), which is non-portable and does not protect other contributors/CI; the repo-local `.gitignore` still needs an explicit `.ralph/logs/` entry.
plan: Add `.ralph/logs/` to `.gitignore` (and keep it adjacent to other `.ralph/` ignores).

Extend `ralph doctor` (`crates/ralph/src/commands/doctor.rs`) to detect when the repo root `.gitignore` is missing an explicit `.ralph/logs/` ignore entry (do not rely on global git excludes) and report a high-severity issue with a concrete fix snippet.
Teach `ralph doctor --auto-fix` to append the ignore entry when missing (idempotent; do not reorder unrelated ignore rules; keep formatting stable).

Add/extend tests in `crates/ralph/tests/doctor_contract_test.rs` to create a temp repo with a minimal `.gitignore`, run `ralph doctor --format json`, assert the missing-ignore issue is emitted, then run `ralph doctor --format json --auto-fix` and assert `.gitignore` contains `.ralph/logs/` and the check reports `fix_applied: true`.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.
notes: Opportunity type: reliability + safety (security).
Why it matters: `.ralph/logs/debug.log` can contain secrets; making it easy to accidentally commit is a high-impact footgun.
Observation: `.gitignore` currently ignores other `.ralph/` state (cache, lock, workspaces) but not `.ralph/logs/`.
Observation: On some machines `.ralph/logs` may be ignored via a global git excludesfile; that is not portable and does not protect other contributors/CI.
Acceptance criteria: `.ralph/logs/` is ignored by default; doctor flags and auto-fixes missing ignore; docs remain consistent.
Ignore .ralph/logs/ in repo .gitignore to prevent committing raw debug logs
Doctor now flags missing .ralph/logs/ ignore (Error) and can auto-fix via --auto-fix
ralph init ensures .ralph/logs/ and .ralph/workspaces/ entries in .gitignore (idempotent)
Integration tests cover doctor detection + auto-fix for missing logs ignore
Docs wording updated to 'ensure .ralph/logs/ is in .gitignore'; make ci passes

tasks.title: Explain why no tasks are runnable (queue explain + run --dry-run)
evidence: CLI: `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/next.rs` prints the next runnable task ID, but when nothing is runnable it prints the next available ID (via `next_id_across`) without explaining why no task is runnable.
CLI: `crates/ralph/src/cli/run.rs` defines `ralph run one` and `ralph run loop`, but neither supports `--dry-run` for selection-only introspection.

Runtime: `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs` logs "All runnable tasks are blocked by unmet dependencies." whenever any todo/draft exists but none is runnable; this can be misleading when the true blocker is `scheduled_start` being in the future.

Core logic: `crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/query.rs` enforces both dependency and `scheduled_start` constraints (`is_task_runnable`), but there is no API/CLI that reports per-task non-runnable reasons (deps vs schedule vs status).
plan: Add a shared "runnability reasons" helper (new module or extend `crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/query.rs`) that returns structured non-runnable reasons per task (unmet deps, scheduled in future, status not runnable).

Implement `ralph queue explain` as a new subcommand (`crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/explain.rs` + wire into `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/mod.rs`) with text output by default and `--format json` for scripting (stable shape).

Extend `ralph queue next` (`crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/next.rs`) with `--explain` to print the chosen task (if any) and, when none is runnable, print the first blocking reason plus a hint to run `ralph queue explain`.

Add `--dry-run` to `ralph run one` and `ralph run loop` (`crates/ralph/src/cli/run.rs` + `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs`) to perform selection + print rationale without invoking any runner and without modifying `.ralph/queue.json` / `.ralph/done.json`.
Fix misleading "blocked by unmet dependencies" messaging in `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs` to reflect both dependency and scheduling blockers (or explicitly point users to `queue explain`).

Add tests covering success + failure modes: blocked-by-deps, scheduled-in-future, only-drafts (without `--include-draft`), and empty-queue. Put core logic tests under `crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/tests/`, and CLI help/flag parsing tests under `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/tests.rs` + `crates/ralph/src/cli/run.rs`.
Update `docs/cli.md` with examples for `queue explain`, `queue next --explain`, and `run --dry-run`, then verify with `make ci`.
notes: Opportunity type: UX improvement + reliability.
Why it matters: When automation stalls, users need deterministic, local explanations to recover quickly (no guesswork, no runner cost).
Repo observation: The current "no runnable tasks" message in `run` can be incorrect when `scheduled_start` is the actual blocker, increasing confusion during recovery.
Acceptance criteria: `queue explain` and `run --dry-run` provide actionable reasons (deps vs schedule vs status); JSON output is stable for scripting; docs/help include examples.
Add runnability report helper (deps/schedule/status) with stable JSON output.
Add 'ralph queue explain' and 'ralph queue next --explain' for actionable selection/blocker diagnostics.
Add 'ralph run one --dry-run' and 'ralph run loop --dry-run' for selection-only introspection (no lock, no queue/done writes).
Fix selection semantics to respect scheduled_start for draft/target selection and improve 'no runnable tasks' messaging.
Document new commands/flags in docs/cli.md; extend tests for success and failure modes.

tasks.title: Add `ralph queue import` (CSV/TSV/JSON) to complement export
evidence: Code: `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/mod.rs` defines `QueueCommand` variants (including `export`) but there is no `import` subcommand today.

Code: `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/shared.rs` + `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/export.rs` implement `queue export` formats `csv|tsv|json|md|gh`; JSON export is a JSON array of `Task` objects and CSV/TSV export uses a fixed header (id/title/status/priority/tags/scope/evidence/plan/notes/request/timestamps/depends_on/custom_fields).
Docs: `docs/cli.md` documents `ralph queue export` but has no `ralph queue import` section/examples.
Constraints: `crates/ralph/src/queue/validation.rs` requires `created_at` and `updated_at` for all tasks, and `completed_at` for `done`/`rejected`; import must backfill/normalize before writing.

Helpers: `crates/ralph/src/queue.rs` (`acquire_queue_lock`, `load_and_validate_queues`, `next_id_across`, `save_queue`) and `crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/mutation.rs` (`backfill_missing_fields`, `backfill_terminal_completed_at`) are the natural primitives to build on.
plan: Add `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/import.rs` implementing `ralph queue import` with `--format <csv|tsv|json>`, `--input <path>` (default: stdin), `--dry-run`, and `--on-duplicate <fail|skip|rename>`; wire it into `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/mod.rs` (`QueueCommand::Import`).

JSON import (MVP): accept the same shape as `ralph queue export --format json` (a JSON array of task objects). Optionally support a `{ "version": 1, "tasks": [...] }` wrapper if it’s trivial and unambiguous.

CSV/TSV import: prioritize round-tripping the current `queue export` CSV/TSV header (id/title/status/priority/tags/scope/evidence/plan/notes/request/created_at/updated_at/completed_at/depends_on/custom_fields). Allow missing optional columns, but require at least `title`; parse list fields using the same delimiters export uses (tags/scope/depends_on comma-separated; evidence/plan/notes `;`-separated; custom_fields `k=v` comma-separated).

Normalization/backfill: for each imported task, trim and drop empty list items; generate missing IDs via `queue::next_id_across`; set missing `created_at`/`updated_at` to `now_utc`; for `done`/`rejected` tasks, set missing `completed_at` to `now_utc` (or gate behind a flag if needed). Reuse `queue::operations::mutation::{backfill_missing_fields, backfill_terminal_completed_at}` where it fits.

Persistence: acquire the queue lock (`queue::acquire_queue_lock`), load+validate active+done (`queue::load_and_validate_queues`), apply duplicate policy against both queues, insert tasks near the top (preserving an in-progress `doing` task using `suggest_new_task_insert_index`/`reposition_new_tasks`), then `queue::save_queue` and re-validate with `validate_queue_set`.

Tests: add focused tests close to the implementation (unit tests in `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/import.rs` plus CLI help contract coverage in `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/tests.rs`). Cover success + failure modes for each format: invalid JSON/CSV, missing required fields, duplicate ID handling modes, timestamp/backfill behavior, and validation failures for bad status/priority/timestamps.
Docs/help: add `ralph queue import --help` examples (stdin + file input + dry-run + duplicate policy) and update `docs/cli.md` (and `docs/queue-and-tasks.md` if it describes import/export workflows).
Gate: run `make ci`.
notes: Opportunity type: feature gap + UX improvement.
Why it matters: Import completes the export workflow and enables bulk backlog seeding and cross-repo task migration without hand-editing JSON.

Implementation note: `queue export --format json` outputs a JSON array of tasks (not a full queue file), and queue validation requires `created_at`/`updated_at` (and `completed_at` for terminal statuses), so import must normalize/backfill before writing.

Acceptance criteria: `queue import` round-trips export JSON/CSV/TSV, is validation-safe (queue+done uniqueness + required timestamps), supports dry-run + duplicate policy, and is fully tested for success + failure modes.
Add ralph queue import (csv/tsv/json) with duplicate policy + timestamp/id backfill
Dry-run now validates resulting queue set; fails on invalid timestamps/dependencies
Fix rename policy to correctly record old->new ID mappings
Docs updated: docs/cli.md + docs/queue-and-tasks.md import section + examples
Tests added/updated for JSON/CSV/TSV import, duplicate handling, and dry-run behavior

tasks.title: Record execution_history for CLI runs (not just TUI) to unlock ETA
evidence: File: `crates/ralph/src/execution_history.rs` defines `record_execution(...)`, writing to `cache_dir.join("execution_history.json")` (i.e., `.ralph/cache/execution_history.json`).

Repo evidence: `rg -n "record_execution\(" crates/ralph/src` shows call sites in `crates/ralph/src/execution_history.rs` (unit tests) and `crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs:446`; `rg -n "record_execution\(" crates/ralph/src/commands/run` returns no matches, so CLI `ralph run one` / `ralph run loop` does not record execution history today.

Repo evidence: CLI post-run supervision is invoked from phase executors: `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/phase2.rs` and `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/single.rs` call `post_run_supervise(...)` on the final iteration, and `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/phase3.rs` calls `post_run_supervise(...)` from `finalize_phase3_if_done(...)` when a task is `Done`.

Repo evidence: `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/shared.rs:execute_runner_pass(...)` is the shared runner execution helper for phases 1/2/single, but phase 3 uses `runutil::run_prompt_with_handling(...)` directly; timing instrumentation must cover both paths to get complete per-phase durations.
Repo state: `.ralph/cache/` exists, but `.ralph/cache/execution_history.json` is absent in this repo, so CLI-first workflows cannot accumulate ETA history without adding recording outside the TUI.
plan: Define CLI timing semantics: record wall-clock durations per phase (Planning/Implementation/Review or SinglePhase) and total duration; decide whether CI gate time is included (recommendation: exclude CI gate and only time runner execution + resume attempts for that phase).

Introduce a small timing accumulator type (e.g., `RunExecutionTimings`) that can collect `HashMap<ExecutionPhase, Duration>` plus `total_duration`, and thread it through the CLI run path (recommended insertion points: `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/shared.rs` + call sites in `phase2.rs`, `single.rs`, and `phase3.rs`).

Instrument runner execution in phases: wrap `execute_runner_pass(...)` with `Instant::now()` timing for phase 1/2/single, and similarly time phase 3's `runutil::run_prompt_with_handling(...)` path so review time is captured.

Cover resume loops: when CI gate continue resumes a session (`supervision::resume_continue_session(...)`), attribute the resumed runner wall-clock time to the same phase bucket so long fix loops are reflected accurately.

Persist history only on real completion: in `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/supervision/mod.rs:post_run_supervise`, after the task is confirmed `Done`, call `crate::execution_history::record_execution(...)` with the accumulated timings and `.ralph/cache` dir; skip recording in `PostRunMode::ParallelWorker`.

Resolve runner/model metadata explicitly: CLI supports per-phase settings (phase matrix), but execution history is keyed by a single `(runner, model, phase_count)` today; decide and implement a deterministic rule (recommended: require runner/model to be consistent across phases or else skip recording with a warning).
Keep parity: refactor `crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs` to reuse the same shared persistence helper so TUI and CLI write identical entry shapes and pruning behavior.

Add tests near the behavior: unit tests for the shared persistence helper (writes on Done; no-write on non-Done; no-write on parallel worker mode) and a focused integration test that constructs timings and validates `.ralph/cache/execution_history.json` is created/appended with stable keys.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.
notes: Opportunity type: UX improvement + reliability.
Why it matters: Predictable iteration speed depends on accumulating real historical run durations across all entrypoints.

Observation (current): execution history is written only from the TUI path (`crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs`), even though CLI run finalization routes through `post_run_supervise(...)` in the phases module; `.ralph/cache/execution_history.json` is still absent in this repo.

Observation (current): `crates/ralph/src/execution_history.rs` and `crates/ralph/src/eta_calculator.rs` already have unit tests, so the missing piece is wiring + timing in the CLI run path (not the persistence/ETA math).
Acceptance criteria: After CLI runs, `.ralph/cache/execution_history.json` exists and ETA calculations have data; tests cover creation + append behavior and no-write on non-Done outcomes.
CLI runs now append .ralph/cache/execution_history.json when (and only when) the task is archived Done
Removed unsafe raw-pointer timing plumbing; now uses RefCell timing sink passed through phases
Recorded runner wall-clock time for initial passes and CI resume continues across phases (including Phase 3)
Added focused unit tests for CLI execution-history recording (record/skip/append/mixed runner-model)

tasks.title: Expose ETA estimates in CLI (queue list/next/stats) using execution_history
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/execution_history.rs` persists execution history to `.ralph/cache/execution_history.json`, and CLI runs append entries via `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/execution_history_cli.rs` (Done-only semantics).

`crates/ralph/src/eta_calculator.rs` loads history from a cache dir (`EtaCalculator::load`) and formats durations (`format_eta`); the TUI stores the latest estimate in `crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs` as `current_eta`.
`ralph queue next --help` exposes only `--with-title` and `--explain` (no ETA output).
`ralph queue list --help` supports `--format compact|long|json` plus filtering/scheduling, but no ETA-related flag/column; `ralph queue stats --help` also has no execution_history/ETA section.
plan: Decide the CLI output contract: add opt-in `--with-eta` for `ralph queue next` and `ralph queue list` (text formats), and keep `ralph queue list --format json` unchanged; if JSON-with-ETA is needed, add a new explicit format (e.g., `--format eta_json`).

Add a shared helper to compute a not-started task ETA from execution history: resolve `(runner, model)` from `task.agent` falling back to `resolved.config.agent`, use `phase_count = resolved.config.agent.phases`, load history from `resolved.repo_root` + `.ralph/cache`, and compute a total estimate; return `None` when there is no history for that key.
Implement `ralph queue next --with-eta`: when a runnable task exists, print an extra ETA column (and optionally confidence indicator); when missing history, print `n/a`.

Implement `ralph queue list --with-eta`: for `--format compact|long`, append an ETA column per row; decide whether ETA is computed for all tasks or only todo/runnable tasks and document it in help/examples.

Extend `ralph queue stats` to include an execution_history-based section (text) and fields (json): sample count plus an estimated total duration for the current configured `(runner, model, phases)`; keep existing keys stable.

Add integration tests under `crates/ralph/tests/` that create a temp repo with `.ralph/cache/execution_history.json`, run `ralph queue next/list/stats` with the new flags, and assert both seeded-history output and `n/a` behavior; update `docs/cli.md` and confirm `make ci` passes.
notes: Opportunity type: UX improvement.
Why it matters: ETA in CLI makes it easier to choose between tasks, plan time blocks, and detect regressions in iteration speed.
Output contract note: `ralph queue list --format json` is documented as the queue export shape; ETA should be opt-in via new flags/formats rather than changing default JSON output.
Implementation note: `EtaCalculator::calculate_eta` can return `Duration::ZERO` for not-started tasks when there is no history; treat missing history as `n/a` instead of showing `0s`.
Acceptance criteria: ETA is visible and accurate for seeded history; missing history is clearly communicated as `n/a`; docs/help include examples.
Add --with-eta for queue next/list (text formats) and execution-history ETA section for queue stats (text+json).
Fix queue next output to keep ETA in same line/column; keep list JSON export unchanged.
Centralize integration test fixtures in crates/ralph/tests/test_support.rs and add ETA integration tests.
Stabilize webhook signature integration test polling; keep CI reliable; make ci passes.

tasks.title: Persist runner/model actually used on completion to improve analytics (by_runner stats)
evidence: File: `crates/ralph/src/reports.rs` computes `velocity.by_runner` and `slow_groups.by_runner` only when `task.agent.runner` is present (`if let Some(agent) = &task.agent && let Some(runner) = &agent.runner { ... }`), and it does not consult `custom_fields`, so most tasks are skipped for by-runner analytics.

Repo evidence: `.ralph/done.json` currently has 55 tasks, 0 tasks with an `agent` field, and no `custom_fields` keys related to runner/model (e.g., no `runner_used`/`model_used`), so `by_runner` breakdowns are empty/underreported today.

Supervised completion: `crates/ralph/src/completions.rs::CompletionSignal` carries only `task_id/status/notes`; `crates/ralph/src/cli/task/status.rs` writes the signal, and `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/phase3.rs::apply_phase3_completion_signal` applies it via `queue::complete_task(...)`, which archives the task without stamping any runner/model metadata.

Parallel completion: `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/merge_runner.rs` also calls `apply_phase3_completion_signal` in a base-sync workspace, so runner/model metadata must survive across workspace boundaries (signal payload or other portable artifact).
plan: Define the persistence contract: keep `task.agent` as a per-task override (selection intent), and store observational analytics fields in `custom_fields` (e.g., `runner_used`, `model_used`, optionally `reasoning_effort_used` and `phase_count_used`).

Make runner/model available to the agent process that runs `ralph task done`: add env vars (e.g., `RALPH_RUNNER_USED`, `RALPH_MODEL_USED`) and set them when launching runner subprocesses (`crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/`) and parallel workers (`crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/worker.rs`).

Extend `CompletionSignal` (`crates/ralph/src/completions.rs`) with optional `runner_used`/`model_used` (and any other chosen analytics fields); update `crates/ralph/src/cli/task/status.rs` to include these fields (read from env) when writing completion signals.

Persist analytics fields on archive: update the Phase 3 application path (`crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/phase3.rs::apply_phase3_completion_signal`) and the parallel merge path to stamp `custom_fields.runner_used`/`custom_fields.model_used` into the task before it is moved into `.ralph/done.json` (likely by extending `queue::complete_task` in `crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/status.rs` to accept a custom_fields patch).

Update `crates/ralph/src/reports.rs` to compute `by_runner` (and slow groups) from `custom_fields.runner_used` when present, falling back to `task.agent.runner` for backward compatibility; normalize keys (lowercase) for stable grouping.

Tests + docs: adjust existing Phase 3 completion signal tests (`crates/ralph/src/commands/run/tests.rs`) to assert custom_fields are persisted; add report/stat tests for `by_runner` using `runner_used`; update `docs/queue-and-tasks.md` and `docs/cli.md` to document the new `custom_fields` keys and when they are written. Validate with `make ci`.
notes: Opportunity type: reliability + cost/observability (analytics).
Why it matters: Without recording what actually ran, productivity and performance reports cannot answer basic questions like "which runner is fastest/most reliable?"

Current state: `reports.rs` by-runner breakdowns are effectively empty because `.ralph/done.json` tasks have no `agent.runner` today; fixing this requires separate observational fields, not reusing `task.agent`.

Implementation note: `queue archive` (`crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/archive.rs`) moves terminal tasks without runner/model context, and the parallel merge runner applies completion signals in a separate base-sync workspace; carry metadata via completion signals (or other portable artifacts) and stamp it at completion/archive time.

Acceptance criteria: Archived tasks persist `custom_fields.runner_used`/`custom_fields.model_used`; `ralph queue stats --format json` includes a non-empty `velocity.by_runner` when done tasks contain those fields; docs describe the recorded fields and clarify they are analytics-only.
Persist runner/model actually used via env -> completion signal -> task.custom_fields
Reports now group by runner_used (fallback agent.runner) for velocity/slow groups
Phase 3 applies analytics patch and keeps signal if patching fails
Added unit tests for runner_used grouping; make ci passes

tasks.title: Add parent/child task navigation (queue tree + task children) using parent_id
evidence: Contract: `schemas/queue.schema.json` and `crates/ralph/src/contracts/task.rs` define optional `parent_id` on tasks.

Behavior: `crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/mutation.rs` sets `child.parent_id = Some(<source task id>)` when splitting a task into children; `crates/ralph/src/cli/task/split.rs` also prints the parent linkage in `--dry-run` output.

I/O: `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/import.rs` supports importing `parent_id`; `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/export.rs` currently does not export `parent_id` (no `parent_id` column/field in the exporters yet).

Validation/UI: `crates/ralph/src/queue/validation.rs` validates RFC3339 timestamps and `depends_on` DAGs, but does not validate `parent_id` referential integrity or detect `parent_id` cycles; there are also no CLI/TUI navigation commands based on `parent_id` today (only `ralph queue graph` for `depends_on`).
plan: Add a queue-level hierarchy helper (new module under `crates/ralph/src/queue/`) to index tasks by `parent_id` across active + done, preserving deterministic ordering (queue file order) and safely handling missing references and cycles (bounded depth / visited set).

Extend `crates/ralph/src/queue/validation.rs` with non-blocking warnings for `parent_id` issues: missing parent reference and cycle detection (do not fail validation yet; report warnings similar to dependency warnings).

Add `ralph task children <TASK_ID> [--include-done] [--recursive] [--format compact|long|json]` implemented under `crates/ralph/src/cli/task/` to list child tasks where `parent_id == TASK_ID` (use the hierarchy helper; keep stable ordering).
Add `ralph task parent <TASK_ID> [--include-done] [--format compact|long|json]` to show the direct parent (if any) and sibling count (children of the same parent).

Add `ralph queue tree [--root <TASK_ID>] [--include-done] [--max-depth <N>]` under `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/` to render an ASCII hierarchy tree based on `parent_id` (explicitly distinct from `ralph queue graph`, which visualizes `depends_on`).
Optional but recommended for I/O parity: extend `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/export.rs` to include `parent_id` in CSV/TSV/JSON exports.
Add minimal TUI parity: show `parent_id` and child count in the task header/details, and explicitly document any remaining gaps between CLI and TUI.
Add tests covering: no children, multiple levels, missing parent reference, `parent_id` cycles, stable ordering, and JSON output stability for the new commands.
Update docs (`docs/queue-and-tasks.md`, `docs/cli.md`) with examples and a clear explanation of hierarchy (`parent_id`) vs dependency graphs (`depends_on`).
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.
notes: Opportunity type: workflow coverage + UX improvement.
Why it matters: Parent/child structure enables epics and decomposition workflows without overloading dependency graphs.

Current state: `parent_id` is part of the task contract and is written by `task split`; docs mention it in `docs/cli.md`, but it is not currently validated or used for navigation (and `queue export` does not export it yet), so missing references/cycles must be handled defensively.

Acceptance criteria: Users can navigate split tasks deterministically (CLI + minimal TUI parity); tree rendering handles edge cases safely; docs reflect the new commands and clearly differentiate hierarchy vs dependencies.
Added parent/child navigation via parent_id: ralph queue tree, ralph task children, ralph task parent (compact/long/json; recursive children).
Hierarchy traversal is deterministic and cycle-safe; queue tree renders cycle-only graphs and children --format json --recursive no longer risks infinite recursion.
Queue validation emits non-blocking warnings for parent_id issues (missing parent, self-parent, cycles).
Export parity: CSV/TSV exports include parent_id; docs updated; TUI task details show parent_id + child count.
Tests updated/added for hierarchy + validation; make ci passes.

tasks.title: Add `ralph plugin init` to scaffold a plugin (plugin.json + scripts)
evidence: Command: `cargo run -p ralph -- plugin --help` lists subcommands list/validate/install/uninstall and has no `init` scaffolding command today.
Docs: `docs/plugin-development.md` describes manual plugin directory creation (write plugin.json + scripts) and does not mention a scaffold command.

Code: plugin discovery only searches `.ralph/plugins/<plugin_id>/plugin.json` (project) and `~/.config/ralph/plugins/<plugin_id>/plugin.json` (global), so a scaffold should generate that on-disk layout by default.

Code: `crates/ralph/src/plugins/manifest.rs` enforces manifest invariants (api_version=1, id must be non-empty/no path separators, processor hooks must be one of validate_task/pre_prompt/post_run), so scaffolding should generate a manifest that passes validation.
plan: Add `plugin init` to the CLI (`crates/ralph/src/cli/plugin.rs`) with help/examples, e.g. `ralph plugin init <PLUGIN_ID> --scope project|global [--path <DIR>] [--with-runner] [--with-processor] [--name ...] [--version ...] [--description ...] [--dry-run]`.

Implement `cmd_init` in `crates/ralph/src/commands/plugin/mod.rs`: choose a default target directory under the discovery roots (`.ralph/plugins/<id>/` for project, `~/.config/ralph/plugins/<id>/` for global), write `plugin.json` using `PluginManifest` + `PLUGIN_API_VERSION`, and optionally scaffold `runner.sh` / `processor.sh` skeletons (no auto-enable; fail if target exists unless `--force`).
Ensure scaffold output passes `PluginManifest::validate()` and is discoverable by `discover_plugins` (directory name matches id; `plugin.json` exists).
Add `--dry-run` behavior and error cases: invalid id (path separators), target exists, unknown scope, and missing HOME for global scope.

Add integration tests in `crates/ralph/tests/` that run `ralph plugin init` in a temp repo and then run `ralph plugin validate --id <id>` to confirm the generated plugin validates; cover dry-run (no files written) and invalid id failure modes.

Update docs: add `ralph plugin init` as the default starting point in `docs/plugin-development.md`, add a short plugin section in `docs/cli.md` (including init examples), and ensure `docs/configuration.md` references the enablement workflow.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.
notes: Opportunity type: integration + UX improvement.
Why it matters: A scaffolder makes the plugin system practical for real teams and reduces manifest/protocol mistakes.
Observation: `docs/cli.md` does not currently document `ralph plugin ...` commands; adding `plugin init` is a good time to document the plugin CLI surface there.
Acceptance criteria: Generated plugin validates; help/examples are useful; scaffold defaults align with discovery roots; docs point to `plugin init` as the default starting point.
Add  scaffolder (project/global scope, optional runner/processor scripts, dry-run/force).
Plugin init outputs a manifest that passes No plugins to validate. and aligns with discovery roots.
Document plugin CLI surface + plugin init quick start in docs.
Integration tests cover success + failure modes (dry-run, invalid id, existing dir, global HOME).
