tasks.title: Enrich webhook payloads and add loop/phase events for better integrations
evidence: File: `crates/ralph/src/webhook.rs` defines `WebhookPayload` with `event`, `timestamp`, `task_id`, `task_title`, plus optional `previous_status`, `current_status`, and `note`; it does not include runner/model/phase/duration or git/CI metadata.
Docs: `docs/configuration.md` lists supported webhook events as task-only: `task_created`, `task_started`, `task_completed`, `task_failed`, `task_status_changed`.

Config: `WebhookConfig.events` filtering treats `events: null` as allow-all (unit test asserts `is_event_enabled(\"any_event\")` is true), so adding new event types would be delivered by default unless filtering semantics change.

Tests: `crates/ralph/tests/webhook_integration_test.rs` already stubs a local HTTP listener and validates non-blocking send, signing header presence/shape, backpressure policy behavior, and event filtering.
CLI: `crates/ralph/src/cli/webhook.rs` only supports the 5 task_* events and does not print the exact JSON body it posts (only URL/event/task id).
Docs gap: `docs/cli.md` currently contains no webhook command documentation.
plan: Decide payload shape for non-task events (loop/phase) since `WebhookPayload` is currently task-centric (required `task_id`/`task_title`). Prefer: keep existing task keys for task_* events, and add optional structured context for loop/phase metadata without forcing fake task IDs.

Extend webhook payload with backward-compatible optional metadata fields (only when known): `runner`, `model`, `phase`, `phase_count`, `duration_ms`, `repo_root`, `branch`, `commit`, and `ci_gate` (e.g., `skipped|passed|failed`). Wire sources from run/phase context (`crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/*`) and git helpers (`crates/ralph/src/git/*`) as needed.

Add new event types: `loop_started`, `loop_stopped`, and `phase_started`/`phase_completed`. Ensure they are opt-in for existing configs by adjusting default event filtering (today `events: null` allows any event string); preserve `events: [\"*\"]` meaning subscribe to all events including new ones.

Emit loop and phase events from execution paths: loop driver(s) (`crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs`, `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs`) and phase runners (`crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/mod.rs`). Include phase durations and CI gate outcome where available (`crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/shared.rs`, `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/supervision/ci.rs`).
Update `ralph webhook test` to support the new event types and add a flag to print the exact JSON payload that will be sent (useful for integration debugging).

Update tests: extend `crates/ralph/tests/webhook_integration_test.rs` to parse the received JSON body and assert new optional fields and new event types behave as specified (including opt-in semantics and signature header stability). Add unit tests in `crates/ralph/src/webhook.rs` for payload serialization and event filtering defaults.
Update documentation: `docs/configuration.md` supported event list and payload examples; add a webhook section to `docs/cli.md` documenting `ralph webhook test` (including new flags/events).
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.
notes: Opportunity type: integration.
Why it matters: Richer, more granular webhooks enable automation (Slack/Discord, dashboards, CI hooks) without parsing logs or scraping state.
Current state: webhook delivery/signature/backpressure/event-filtering already have both unit and integration coverage (see `crates/ralph/tests/webhook_integration_test.rs`).
Design note: making new event types truly opt-in likely requires changing default `events` filtering behavior (today `events: null` is effectively allow-all).
Acceptance criteria: Payload remains backward compatible for existing task_* events; new events are opt-in; tests cover serialization/signature, delivery, and opt-in semantics.
Added loop_started/loop_stopped and phase_started/phase_completed webhook events with enriched, backward-compatible payload context.
Changed webhook event filtering defaults so new loop_/phase_ events are opt-in; ['*'] still subscribes to all.
Enhanced ralph webhook test: supports new events and can print exact JSON via --print-json with --pretty true|false.
Expanded unit+integration coverage (JSON body assertions, opt-in semantics) and split webhook unit tests into a submodule to keep source files under size heuristics.
Verified via make ci (format, clippy, tests, build, schema generation).

tasks.title: Add `ralph config show --format json` for scripting and tooling
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/cli/config.rs`: `ConfigCommand::Show` currently prints pretty JSON via `serde_json::to_string_pretty(&resolved.config)` and has no output format switch.
`crates/ralph/src/cli/config.rs`: `config show` help text still claims "(YAML)", so `ralph config show --help` is misleading relative to the actual output.
`docs/cli.md` currently does not document `ralph config ...`, despite being the canonical CLI reference.
There is already an established `--format <text|json>` pattern in the CLI (e.g. `ralph doctor --format json`, `ralph queue stats --format json`).
plan: Introduce `ralph config show --format <yaml|json>` (Clap `ValueEnum`), and decide a default: recommended `yaml` for humans with explicit `--format json` for scripts.
Make `config show` accept args (e.g., `ConfigCommand::Show(ConfigShowArgs)`), and ensure `--help` includes examples for both formats.
Implement rendering: JSON should remain the resolved config as a JSON object; YAML should render the same resolved config structure (no extra wrapper) for parity.

Add tests covering both formats and failure modes: `--format json` parses as JSON and contains expected top-level keys; `--format yaml` parses as YAML and contains the same keys; invalid `--format` values fail with a clear message.
Update `docs/cli.md` to add a `ralph config` section (including `show`, `paths`, `schema`) and include scripting examples for `--format json`.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.
notes: Opportunity type: UX improvement + integration.
Current state: `ralph config show` already emits JSON, but help text claims YAML and there is no explicit format flag (harder to build stable scripts and harder for humans to read).
Acceptance criteria: `ralph config show --format json` is stable for parsing; YAML is available for readability if selected/defaulted; help and `docs/cli.md` match behavior.
Added --format <yaml|json> to ralph config show; default is yaml for humans
JSON output remains the resolved config object for stable scripting
Updated CLI help/examples + docs/cli.md ralph config section
Added integration tests for yaml/json formats + invalid format

tasks.title: Expand `ralph queue list --sort-by` to support timestamps and scheduling fields
evidence: Command: `ralph queue list --help` currently supports `--sort-by priority` only.

Command: `ralph queue list` already supports scheduling filters (`--scheduled`, `--scheduled-after`, `--scheduled-before`) on `scheduled_start`, but there is no `--sort-by scheduled_start` (or other timestamps) for time-based triage.

Code: `QueueSortBy` (in `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/shared.rs`) currently only contains `priority` and is shared by both `queue list` and `queue sort`, so adding more fields requires either implementing them for both commands or splitting list vs sort enums.
Tests: `crates/ralph/tests/queue_sorting_cli_test.rs` currently asserts clap errors list only `priority` as the valid sort-by value for both `queue list` and `queue sort`.
plan: Split sort-by enums so `queue list` can grow without widening `queue sort` reordering semantics: keep `QueueSortBy` (priority-only) for `ralph queue sort`, introduce a dedicated `QueueListSortBy` for `ralph queue list`.

Implement `ralph queue list --sort-by` for: `priority` (existing), `created_at`, `updated_at`, `started_at`, `scheduled_start`, `status`, and `title`; use `crate::timeutil::parse_rfc3339` for timestamp comparison and always include a stable tie-breaker (e.g., `id`) so output ordering is deterministic.
Define and document ordering rules for missing/invalid timestamps (and missing scheduled_start) for both ascending and descending sorts.

Update docs/help: `docs/cli.md` and `ralph queue list --help` should list all supported sort fields and include examples for scheduled triage; keep `ralph queue sort --help` limited to priority and document the distinction.

Update tests: extend `crates/ralph/tests/queue_sorting_cli_test.rs` to expect new list sort-by values while keeping sort command limited to priority; add ordering tests for each new list sort field (including missing timestamp cases) and update any affected unit tests in `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/tests.rs`.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.
notes: Opportunity type: UX improvement.
Why it matters: Better sorting reduces friction in daily triage and makes CLI workflows competitive with the TUI for backlog management.
Acceptance criteria: Sort results are correct and stable; edge cases (missing timestamps) are well-defined; tests cover all modes.
Observation: scheduling already exists as filters in `queue list`; this task is specifically about adding time-based sorting while keeping `queue sort` (file reordering) conservative.
queue list: add --sort-by priority|created_at|updated_at|started_at|scheduled_start|status|title with stable id tie-breaker
missing/invalid timestamps sort last regardless of order (documented)
queue sort remains priority-only; help/docs clarify list vs sort semantics
docs/tests updated: docs/cli.md + queue_sorting_cli_test coverage for new fields

tasks.title: Publish tasks to GitHub Issues via `gh` (store issue URL in custom_fields)
evidence: Command: `ralph queue export --format gh` is implemented (via `export_github_issue()` in `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/export.rs`) and emits GitHub-issue-friendly Markdown; there is no built-in publish/sync to GitHub Issues yet.
Search: there is no `gh issue ...` integration in the codebase today (only `gh pr ...` usage in `crates/ralph/src/git/pr.rs`).
Pattern: the repo already has a robust `gh` preflight + testability approach in `crates/ralph/src/git/pr.rs` and a fake-`gh`-on-PATH test pattern in `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/state.rs`.

Data model: `Task.custom_fields` is a `HashMap<String, String>` with scalar coercion on deserialize (`crates/ralph/src/contracts/task.rs`), so storing `github_issue_url`/`github_issue_number` as strings is compatible with the current schema.
plan: Add a new queue subcommand (recommended: `ralph queue issue publish <TASK_ID>`) under `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/` and wire it into `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/mod.rs` with `--help` examples.

Implement a small GitHub Issues helper (new module recommended: `crates/ralph/src/git/issue.rs`) that runs `gh issue create` and (when an existing issue is recorded) `gh issue edit`; reuse the `gh` availability/auth preflight from `crates/ralph/src/git/pr.rs`.

Render issue title/body from the task (body should reuse the existing GitHub renderer from `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/export.rs`, e.g., by factoring `export_github_issue()` into a reusable helper for a single task).

Persist the created/updated issue metadata back into the task’s `custom_fields` (at minimum `github_issue_url`; optionally `github_issue_number`) and update the task `updated_at` timestamp in `.ralph/queue.json`.

Support `--dry-run` (print rendered title/body and the `gh` command that would be executed) and passthrough flags like `--label`/`--assignee`; if `github_issue_url` is already present, re-running publish should update the issue title/body instead of creating a new issue.

Add tests for: success path (issue created and queue updated), failure when `gh` is missing, failure when `gh` is present but unauthenticated, and update path when `github_issue_url` already exists (use injected runner or a fake `gh` on PATH).
Update `docs/cli.md` with a workflow: `ralph queue export --format gh --id-pattern RQ-0655` (optional preview), `ralph queue issue publish RQ-0655`, and re-run publish to sync updates.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.
notes: Opportunity type: integration + automation.
Why it matters: Publishing turns Ralph queues into shareable, reviewable work items without duplicating effort across systems.
Acceptance criteria: Issue creation/update works with real `gh`; mapping is persisted; tests cover success + failure (gh missing/auth).

Implementation note: `queue export --format gh` is already implemented and tested; this task is specifically about automating publish/sync via `gh issue` and persisting the mapping into `custom_fields`.
Added ralph queue issue publish to create/update GitHub Issues via gh and persist issue URL/number in task custom_fields.
Added deterministic fake-gh tests covering create/update plus gh missing/auth failure modes; PATH mutations are guarded.
Improved check_gh_available() error text so it is accurate outside parallel mode.
Docs updated in docs/cli.md; make ci passes.

tasks.title: Fix resume flow when queue lock is stale (avoid 50-failure abort loop)
evidence: User report: after "Incomplete session detected" and answering "Resume this session? y", the run fails with "Queue lock already held ... (STALE PID)" and suggests using --force or removing .ralph/lock manually.
User report: the run loop then repeats the same failure until "aborting after 50 consecutive failures", preventing session resume and requiring manual intervention.
crates/ralph/src/session/mod.rs: the resume prompt is printed via "Incomplete session detected" and "Resume this session? [Y/n]".

crates/ralph/src/lock.rs: stale locks are only auto-removed when acquiring with `force=true` (and PID is confirmed dead); otherwise lock acquisition returns an error that includes a `--force`/`rm -rf` suggestion.

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs + crates/ralph/src/constants.rs: `RunLoop` continues after generic errors and aborts only after `MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES` (currently 50), so a deterministic lock error can spin quickly into the 50-failure abort path.

crates/ralph/tests/stale_lock_cleanup_test.rs + crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/unlock.rs + docs/cli.md: the repo already has a tested recovery mechanism (`--force` clears stale locks; `ralph queue unlock` removes the lock dir) that the resume flow can leverage.
plan: Add a regression integration test that simulates: a valid resumable session (.ralph/cache/session.json for a Doing task) plus a stale queue lock dir (.ralph/lock with a dead PID), then asserts `ralph run loop` resumes without requiring manual `rm -rf .ralph/lock` and does not hit the 50-consecutive-failures abort path.

Define the intended behavior for resume with a stale lock: after the user confirms resume, automatically clear the stale lock (equivalent to `force=true` for stale-only) before starting `run_one`, or prompt once to clear via `ralph queue unlock`.

Make lock acquisition failures non-retriable in the run loop: treat "Queue lock already held" as a systemic abort (single actionable error) unless it is stale and can be safely cleared; do not allow it to drive the tight 50-failure retry loop.
Extend tests to cover failure modes: live-PID lock must not be cleared; stale-PID lock can be cleared; unreadable/missing owner metadata yields a single actionable error (no repeated retries).
Update `docs/cli.md` to document the resume + lock interaction and prefer the built-in recovery command (`ralph queue unlock`) over raw `rm -rf` in user-facing guidance where possible.
Run `make ci` and ensure it passes.
notes: Current lock/recovery primitives already exist (`--force` stale-lock cleanup in crates/ralph/src/lock.rs; explicit unlock in crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/unlock.rs); the missing piece is wiring them into the resume path and preventing lock errors from entering the generic 50-failure loop.
Resume flow now pre-clears stale queue lock (force cleanup only for dead PID).
Run loop treats queue lock contention as non-retriable to avoid 50-failure abort spin.
Lock error messaging now prefers  over manual rm -rf and docs describe resume+lock interaction.
Added regression tests for stale-lock cleanup + resume wiring + immediate abort on live lock.

tasks.title: Use real RFC3339 parsing for execution history weighting (fix inaccurate age_days)
evidence: crates/ralph/src/execution_history.rs:188-191: weight = 0.9^(age_in_days) uses `parse_timestamp_to_secs` output to compute `age_days`.
crates/ralph/src/execution_history.rs:229-263: `parse_timestamp_to_secs` does ad-hoc parsing with approximate `days_since_epoch = (year - 1970) * 365 + month * 30 + day`.
crates/ralph/src/timeutil.rs:44-60: `parse_rfc3339` and `parse_rfc3339_opt` functions already exist for proper RFC3339 parsing.
User impact: ETA weighting can skew significantly; older entries may be weighted incorrectly due to leap year and month-length approximations.
plan: Replace `parse_timestamp_to_secs` with `timeutil::parse_rfc3339_opt(timestamp)` and compute age using real unix timestamps (or OffsetDateTime deltas).
Add unit tests comparing parsed seconds to `timeutil::parse_rfc3339(ts)?.unix_timestamp()` for timestamps with and without subseconds.
Add a regression test that entries further in the past get smaller weights (monotonic decay).
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.
notes: Verified: timeutil::parse_rfc3339_opt exists at timeutil.rs:53 and returns Option<OffsetDateTime>.
Verified: execution_history.rs has 444 lines; parse_timestamp_to_secs is at lines 229-263.
The timeutil module is already available as a public mod in lib.rs; execution_history uses crate::timeutil::now_utc_rfc3339() inline at line 121.
Fixed RFC3339 parsing in execution_history.rs: replaced ad-hoc date math with timeutil::parse_rfc3339_opt for accurate age_days calculation
Added 4 unit tests: accuracy vs timeutil, leap year handling, monotonic decay regression, subsecond handling
Added defensive check for pre-epoch timestamps to prevent i64→u64 overflow
All 2,733+ tests pass including new tests
No other files needed changes - codebase already uses centralized timeutil parsing

tasks.title: Stop writing empty timestamps (replace now_utc_rfc3339().unwrap_or_default)
evidence: Command: rg -n 'now_utc_rfc3339\(\)\.unwrap_or_default\(\)' crates/ralph/src -> execution_history.rs:121; productivity.rs:55,207,340 (4 occurrences total).
crates/ralph/src/timeutil.rs:67-69: `now_utc_rfc3339_or_fallback()` exists and uses FALLBACK_RFC3339 constant instead of empty string.
Helper function now_utc_rfc3339_or_fallback() is already used in 50+ call sites across codebase (session/, queue/, commands/run/, etc.).
Failure mode: empty timestamps can be persisted into .ralph/cache files, breaking parsing/sorting and producing confusing UX.
Test coverage exists: execution_history.rs has 14 internal tests, productivity.rs has 17 tests, timeutil.rs has 11 internal + 23 integration tests.

tasks.title: Fail fast on malformed README version markers (don't default to version 1)
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/init/readme.rs:48-60: extract_readme_version returns Some(1) for ALL cases except when marker found and parsed successfully - this treats malformed markers as legacy v1.
crates/ralph/src/commands/init/readme.rs:85: `extract_readme_version(&content).unwrap_or(1)` - call site uses unwrap_or(1) masking any None returns.
crates/ralph/src/commands/init/readme.rs:116: `extract_readme_version(&content).unwrap_or(1)` - second call site also masking potential errors.
Failure mode: A malformed marker like `<!-- RALPH_README_VERSION: invalid -->` returns Some(1) instead of failing, hiding corruption.
Existing tests at lines 168-182 only cover valid marker and no-marker cases - missing malformed marker tests.
plan: Change `extract_readme_version` return type from `Option<u32>` to `Result<u32, ReadmeVersionError>` with error variants for: NoMarker (legacy), InvalidFormat, ParseError.
Update `check_readme_current_from_root` to handle Result: NoMarker -> treat as v1; InvalidFormat/ParseError -> return Err with actionable message.
Update `write_readme` to propagate errors from extract_readme_version instead of silently defaulting to 1.
Add unit tests: invalid marker (non-numeric), malformed marker (missing end), valid marker, no marker cases.
Add integration test that creates a README with malformed marker and verifies the error message is user-friendly.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.
notes: The current implementation always returns Some(1) for any non-parseable marker, making it impossible to detect corruption.
The fix requires distinguishing between: 1) No marker at all (legacy, OK to default), 2) Marker present but malformed (error, should fail fast).
Fixed: extract_readme_version now returns Result<u32, ReadmeVersionError> instead of Option<u32>
Added: ReadmeVersionError enum with NoMarker, InvalidFormat, and ParseError variants
Fixed: All call sites in write_readme now properly propagate malformed marker errors
Added: 6 new unit tests and 6 integration tests for malformed marker handling
Verified: make ci passes with 2200+ tests

evidence: User request: Create foundational plugin system to enable isolating all runners into plugins for easier runner-specific customization.
crates/ralph/src/plugins/manifest.rs:33-44: Current RunnerPlugin manifest only has bin, supports_resume, default_model - lacks execution/response-parsing traits.

crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/response.rs:19-190: Response parsing is hardcoded per runner with runner-specific extract functions.

plan: Design RunnerPlugin trait with methods for: command building (run/resume), CLI option mapping, response parsing, and metadata (default model, supports resume).
Create PluginExecutor that delegates to RunnerPlugin trait implementations instead of hardcoded run_* functions.
Design ResponseParser trait with runner-specific implementations for extracting assistant text from JSON streams.
Create BuiltInRunnerPlugin enum wrapping all 7 built-in runners that implements RunnerPlugin trait.

Implement runner-specific logic for each built-in: codex (exec/resume subcommands, legacy_json), opencode (temp file handling), gemini (--resume flag), claude (-p flag), kimi (--session, --yolo), pi (session path resolution), cursor (phase-aware defaults).
Refactor runner execution dispatch to use plugin-based lookup by Runner enum variant.
Add comprehensive tests for the plugin trait system in crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/tests/.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming all tests pass.
notes: 2026-02-06: Task refreshed. External plugin system already exists (manifest/registry/discovery), but built-in runners are still hardcoded. This task bridges the gap by creating a trait system that lets built-in runners act like plugins.
Key distinction: crates/ralph/src/plugins/ handles external plugin.json-based plugins, while this task creates the trait system for built-in runners in crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/.
Removed ~120 lines of duplicated CLI option functions
Refactored response parsers to centralize JSON parsing
Verified all 73 execution module tests pass
Code compiles without warnings

tasks.title: Preserve timeout diagnostics when output-capture mutex is poisoned
evidence: crates/ralph/src/runutil/execution.rs:199: `if let Ok(mut buf) = capture_for_handler.lock()` silently drops output chunks on PoisonError.
crates/ralph/src/runutil/execution.rs:300: `capture.lock().map(|buf| buf.clone()).unwrap_or_default()` discards PoisonError and returns empty string, losing all captured output.
Both issues affect timeout_stdout_capture: Arc<Mutex<String>> used for timeout diagnostics and safeguard dumps.
crates/ralph/src/runutil/tests.rs: No existing test coverage for mutex poisoning scenarios.
File verified: execution.rs is 572 lines, tests.rs is 412 lines.
plan: In wrap_output_handler_with_capture handler (line 199): Change `if let Ok(mut buf) = ...` to handle PoisonError with `unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())` to recover data from poisoned mutex.
In timeout error handling (line 300): Change `capture.lock().map(...).unwrap_or_default()` to use `unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner().clone())` to preserve captured output on poison.
Add logging (warn!) when mutex poison is detected so operators are aware of the issue.

Add unit test in crates/ralph/src/runutil/tests.rs that: creates an Arc<Mutex<String>>, spawns a thread that panics while holding the lock, then verifies the handler still records output without panicking.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.
notes: Verified 2026-02-06: File line numbers confirmed. execution.rs uses flat module structure (mod entry in runutil.rs).
Fixed mutex poisoning in timeout_stdout_capture: wrap_output_handler_with_capture (line ~198) and timeout error handling (line ~310) now use into_inner() to recover data
Added warning logs when mutex poison is detected for operator visibility
Added unit test timeout_stdout_capture_survives_mutex_poison verifying graceful handling
All 23 runutil tests pass; clippy clean; type-check passes

tasks.title: Implement auto-archive system for completed tasks
tasks.description:
Auto-archiving is now partially implemented (TUI + run supervision), but queue maintenance still relies on manual `ralph queue archive` in some workflows.

Current state:
- `ralph task done` / `ralph task reject` moves the task directly to `.ralph/done.json`.
- TUI supports auto-archive on setting Done/Rejected via `tui.auto_archive_terminal` (`never|prompt|always`).
- `ralph run` post-run supervision calls `queue::archive_terminal_tasks`.

Remaining gap:
- Terminal tasks can still linger in `.ralph/queue.json` (manual edits, TUI with auto-archive=never, or status changes that only stamp `completed_at`). Add a configurable, queue-level sweep that archives terminal tasks automatically (optionally after N days since `completed_at`) and align docs/config naming.
evidence: Manual archive command exists: `ralph queue archive` (docs/cli.md, crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/archive.rs)
Archive implementation: `queue::archive_terminal_tasks` moves Done/Rejected tasks and stamps timestamps (crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/archive.rs)
TUI auto-archive exists via `tui.auto_archive_terminal` (docs/tui-task-management.md, crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs)
`ralph task done` / `ralph task reject` already archives the task by moving it to `.ralph/done.json` (crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/status.rs)
`ralph run` post-run supervision archives terminal tasks automatically (crates/ralph/src/commands/run/supervision/mod.rs)
Gap: QueueConfig has no age-based/periodic auto-archive sweep; terminal tasks can remain in `.ralph/queue.json` unless explicitly archived (schemas/config.schema.json)
plan: Clarify desired semantics and config surface: queue-level sweep (age-based) vs changing defaults; ensure `ralph run` invariants (Phase 3 requires task archived) remain intact
Add `queue.auto_archive_terminal_after_days: Option<u32>` (None=disabled, 0=immediate) to `QueueConfig`, regenerate schema, and document in docs/configuration.md
Implement an age-based archive helper (e.g., `archive_terminal_tasks_older_than_days`) in crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/archive.rs using `completed_at` timestamps
Integrate the sweep into safe entrypoints that already hold the queue lock (e.g., TUI session start and/or other maintenance paths) without breaking `ralph run` completion checks
Update docs: docs/cli.md and docs/tui-task-management.md to explain what is already auto-archived and how the new queue-level sweep works
Add tests covering: config disabled, immediate sweep, age-based sweep, missing/invalid timestamps, and interaction with manual `ralph queue archive`
notes: Repo state (2026-02-07): auto-archive on terminal status is implemented in the TUI (`tui.auto_archive_terminal`), `ralph task done/reject` archives directly, and `ralph run` post-run supervision already archives terminal tasks. This task should focus on any remaining queue-level sweep/delay behavior and docs/config alignment (or be closed if deemed unnecessary).
Implemented queue-level auto-archive sweep via queue.auto_archive_terminal_after_days
Added age-based archive logic (0=immediate, N>0=after N days)
Integrated sweep into TUI reload and CLI task edit operations
Added comprehensive tests covering all sweep scenarios
Updated docs: configuration.md, tui-task-management.md, cli.md

custom_fields.user_value: Eliminates manual step, prevents queue bloat, reduces cognitive load

tasks.title: Activate processor plugin hooks (validate_task, pre_prompt, post_run)
tasks.description:
Processor plugin hooks are defined and documented, but never invoked during `ralph run`.

The manifest supports processor hooks and `ralph plugin init` scaffolds a `processor.sh` script with the hook protocol, yet the run execution flow never calls these hooks. The registry has `enabled_processor_plugins()` and `resolve_processor_bin()` still marked `#[allow(dead_code)]` and there are no call sites.

Goals:
1. Add hook invocation points in task execution flow
2. Call `validate_task` before execution (can reject task with an actionable error)
3. Call `pre_prompt` after prompt compilation but before runner invocation (can modify prompt text)
4. Call `post_run` after runner execution completes (can process output and trigger side effects)
5. Support chaining multiple processors in a documented deterministic order (config is a `BTreeMap` keyed by plugin id)

Evidence:
- Manifest validation allowlists `validate_task`, `pre_prompt`, `post_run` (`crates/ralph/src/plugins/manifest.rs`)
- Processor protocol is scaffolded and documented (`crates/ralph/src/commands/plugin/mod.rs`, `docs/plugin-development.md`)
- No runtime hook invocation exists; processor registry helpers remain unused (`crates/ralph/src/plugins/registry.rs`)
evidence: Manifest: processor hooks are defined and validated (validate_task, pre_prompt, post_run) in `crates/ralph/src/plugins/manifest.rs`
Scaffold + docs: `ralph plugin init --with-processor` generates `processor.sh` with a hook protocol + `--help` examples (`crates/ralph/src/commands/plugin/mod.rs`, `docs/plugin-development.md`)
Registry gap: `crates/ralph/src/plugins/registry.rs` still has `resolve_processor_bin()` and `enabled_processor_plugins()` behind `#[allow(dead_code)]`, and `rg` finds no call sites outside that file
Run flow gap: searched `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/**` and `crates/ralph/src/runner/**`; there is no processor hook invocation (internal `post_run_supervise` is unrelated)
plan: Define processor chaining order semantics (deterministic default vs new explicit config field) and document the chosen behavior
Load `PluginRegistry` for `ralph run` and thread it through the run/phase execution path so runners and processors can resolve plugin bins and config blobs
Implement a `ProcessorExecutor` module to invoke enabled processor plugins by hook (validate_task, pre_prompt, post_run), mirroring the `processor.sh` protocol and passing `RALPH_PLUGIN_CONFIG_JSON`

Invoke `validate_task` before marking the task Doing / starting runner execution: write task JSON to a temp file, run processor(s), abort on non-zero with an actionable error that identifies the failing plugin

Invoke `pre_prompt` after the prompt is built but before `execute_runner_pass`: write prompt to a temp file, run processor(s) sequentially, and read the (possibly modified) prompt back after each processor

Invoke `post_run` after runner output is available and any Continue/CI loop completes: write stdout (NDJSON) to a temp file, run processor(s), and decide whether failures are fatal or warnings (document it)
Remove `#[allow(dead_code)]` from `crates/ralph/src/plugins/registry.rs` processor helpers after they have real call sites; eliminate any now-unnecessary `dead_code` allowances
Add tests covering success and failure modes (hook ordering, validate_task abort, prompt mutation, post_run invocation) using temp plugin dirs + simple processor scripts

Verify: `cargo test -p ralph`, `make ci`, and a manual smoke run with `ralph plugin init my.plugin --with-processor --scope project`, enabling the plugin in `.ralph/config.json`, and observing hook executions
notes: 2026-02-07: Hooks are validated, documented, and scaffolded (processor.sh), but there are zero runtime call sites; processor helpers in `crates/ralph/src/plugins/registry.rs` remain `#[allow(dead_code)]`.
Chaining order is currently underspecified: plugin config is a `BTreeMap` keyed by plugin id, so 'config order' needs an explicit definition if order matters.
Implemented processor plugin hooks (validate_task, pre_prompt, post_run)
Fixed Phase 3 to use execute_runner_pass for consistent hook invocation
Fixed Windows compilation with #[cfg(unix)] guards
All 2191 tests pass, CI clean

custom_fields.user_value: Unlocks full plugin extensibility - custom validators, prompt modifiers, post-processing

tasks.title: Add task aging report to identify stale work
tasks.description:
No way to identify tasks that have been sitting in queue for too long. This leads to forgotten work and queue rot. Add an aging report that surfaces stale tasks by status and age thresholds.

Goals:
1. Add `ralph queue aging` command showing tasks grouped by age buckets
2. Default thresholds: >7 days (warning), >14 days (stale), >30 days (rotten)
3. Configurable thresholds in config
4. Show aging in TUI with visual indicators (color-coded)
5. Support filtering by status (default: todo, doing)

Evidence:
- Current: Queue has timestamps (created_at, started_at) but no aging analysis
- Gap: No visibility into stale tasks
- User pain: Forgotten tasks accumulate, queue bloat
evidence: CLI: `cargo run -p ralph -- queue --help` lists `stats`, `history`, and `burndown` but no `aging` subcommand.
Queue CLI registry in `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/mod.rs` wires report-style commands (`stats`, `history`, `burndown`); there is no `QueueCommand::Aging` yet.
Reports live in `crates/ralph/src/reports.rs` (stats/history/burndown); no aging report implementation exists.
Task timestamps exist for age computation: `schemas/queue.schema.json` defines `created_at`, `started_at`, `updated_at`, `completed_at` (RFC3339 UTC strings).
TUI task list rendering is in `crates/ralph/src/tui/render/panels/list.rs`; current UI shows status/priority/scheduling indicators but not age buckets.
plan: Define aging semantics per status (timestamp source + bucket boundaries): for `todo` use `created_at`; for `doing` prefer `started_at` when present, otherwise `created_at`; use UTC and strict >N day thresholds.

Add configurable thresholds: extend `QueueConfig` in `crates/ralph/src/contracts/config.rs` with an optional `aging_thresholds` struct (warning_days/stale_days/rotten_days). Update `schemas/config.schema.json` and document in `docs/configuration.md`.
Implement aging bucket logic in `crates/ralph/src/reports.rs` using `crates/ralph/src/timeutil.rs` RFC3339 parsing. Decide how to handle missing/invalid timestamps (exclude vs explicit `unknown`).

Add `ralph queue aging` in `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/aging.rs` following the `stats/history/burndown` pattern. Support `--status` filtering (default: todo, doing) and `--format <text|json>` consistent with other queue reports.
Wire the new subcommand in `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/mod.rs` (module, enum variant, handler routing). Update `docs/cli.md` with usage + examples.

Add TUI inline aging indicators in `crates/ralph/src/tui/render/panels/list.rs` (color-coded bucket label or icon). If needed, thread colors through the existing palette (`crates/ralph/src/tui/app_palette.rs`).
Tests: bucket boundary tests and missing/invalid timestamp handling near report logic; CLI tests in `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/tests.rs` for filtering and JSON shape/ordering.
Verify: `cargo run -p ralph -- queue aging`, `cargo run -p ralph -- queue aging --format json`, and a quick TUI pass (`cargo run -p ralph -- tui`) to confirm indicators render.
notes: Repo layout update: reporting code is `crates/ralph/src/reports.rs` (not `crates/ralph/src/queue/reports.rs`).
Repo layout update: task list rendering is `crates/ralph/src/tui/render/panels/list.rs` (not `crates/ralph/src/tui/components/task_list.rs`).
Implemented task aging report feature
Added ralph queue aging command with text/json output
Added configurable thresholds (warning/stale/rotten days) to QueueConfig
Added TUI aging indicators (⏳) with color coding
Added comprehensive tests: 17 unit tests + 4 CLI tests
Updated docs/configuration.md with aging_thresholds documentation
Regenerated schemas/config.schema.json

custom_fields.user_value: Prevents forgotten tasks, improves queue hygiene, surfaces work needing attention

tasks.title: Stop swallowing plugin config JSON serialization errors
tasks.description:
Principles violated: Fail fast (F), Least astonishment (G), Operational hygiene (H).

Evidence shows plugin config blobs are serialized with a silent fallback to "{}" when `serde_json::to_string` fails. This discards the underlying error and causes plugin runners to execute with an empty/default config.

This pattern currently exists in multiple plugin execution entrypoints (prompt + resume).

Why it matters (failure modes):
- Plugin runners can silently misconfigure (wrong model, wrong tool flags, wrong behavior) while Ralph proceeds as if configuration was applied.
- Debugging becomes hard because the error is not surfaced; users see downstream behavior without the root cause.

Minimal proposed fix:
- Replace `serde_json::to_string(&v).unwrap_or_else(|_| "{}".to_string())` with error propagation and context that includes `plugin_id`.
- Use a shared helper so all plugin run/resume paths behave consistently.
- (Related) `crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/plugin.rs` also falls back to "{}" on runner_cli JSON serialization; this is likely infallible today, but the pattern should be removed for consistency.

Acceptance criteria:
- New/updated tests cover the failure path and assert the error includes the plugin id and indicates config serialization failure.
- `make ci` passes.
evidence: crates/ralph/src/runner.rs:600-616 sets plugin_cfg via serde_json::to_string(...).unwrap_or_else(|_| "{}".to_string()) (silent fallback)
crates/ralph/src/runner.rs:653-669 repeats the same silent fallback for resume path
crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/plugin_executor.rs:276-292 serializes plugin_config_blob with the same silent fallback for external plugin run
crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/plugin_executor.rs:311-328 repeats the silent fallback for external plugin resume
crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/plugin.rs:46 and :92 swallow runner_cli JSON serialization errors by falling back to "{}"
Command: `rg -n "serde_json::to_string\(.*\)\\.unwrap_or_else\(\|_\| \"\\{\\}\"" crates/ralph/src`
plan: Inventory all plugin config JSON serialization call sites and extract a shared helper that propagates serde_json errors with plugin_id context
Replace the silent "{}" fallback in `crates/ralph/src/runner.rs` and `crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/plugin_executor.rs` for both run and resume paths
Remove the runner_cli JSON serialization silent fallback in `crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/plugin.rs` (propagate/wrap errors consistently)

Add a focused unit test that forces a `serde_json::to_string` failure (e.g., deeply nested `serde_json::Value`) and asserts Ralph returns an error (no silent "{}" fallback) that includes the plugin id
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph` and `make ci` and confirming they pass
notes: Repo-wide grep shows the same silent fallback pattern in plugin executor and plugin execution helper modules; the fix should be centralized to avoid drift.
Fixed: Replaced silent '{}' fallback with error propagation using shared helper serialize_plugin_env_json
Coverage: 3 tests added covering plugin config and runner CLI serialization failure/success paths
Verified: No swallow patterns remain (rg check), all tests pass, CI passes
Files: plugin.rs (helper + tests), mod.rs (re-export), runner.rs (2 call sites), plugin_executor.rs (2 call sites)

tasks.title: Make RFC3339 now fallback explicit to prevent silent timestamp corruption
tasks.description:
Principles violated: Fail fast (F), Operational hygiene (H), Consistency/Integrity (J).

`now_utc_rfc3339_or_fallback()` silently returns a hard-coded `FALLBACK_RFC3339` string on formatting errors. This value is a plausible real timestamp (currently `2026-01-18...`), which can silently corrupt data and mask timeout logic.

Why it matters (failure modes):
- If formatting fails, queue/history timestamps become a fixed value that looks real, skewing reports/ETAs and making debugging difficult.
- Session timeout checks can be skipped or miscomputed: `validate_session` parses now via the fallback and then `unwrap_or(session_time)`, potentially disabling timeout detection in the presence of errors.

Minimal proposed fix (API-stable):
- When the fallback path is used, emit a `log::error!` (or `warn!`) with the underlying error; do not silently swallow it.
- Change `FALLBACK_RFC3339` to an unmistakable sentinel value (e.g., `1970-01-01T00:00:00.000000000Z`) so fallback usage is obvious in persisted data.
- In `session::validate_session`, avoid the string roundtrip for now; use `OffsetDateTime::now_utc()` directly for comparisons, and only format to RFC3339 at IO boundaries.

Acceptance criteria:
- Unit tests cover session timeout behavior using deterministic time inputs (e.g., refactor validation to accept `now: OffsetDateTime` in an internal helper).
- Fallback constant is a clear sentinel and is only used when a logged error occurs.
- `make ci` passes.
evidence: crates/ralph/src/timeutil.rs:67-69 now_utc_rfc3339_or_fallback discards the formatting error (|_|), returns FALLBACK_RFC3339, and does not log
crates/ralph/src/constants.rs:205 defines FALLBACK_RFC3339 as 2026-01-18T00:00:00.000000000Z (plausible timestamp)
crates/ralph/src/session/mod.rs:121-136 parses now via now_utc_rfc3339_or_fallback and then unwrap_or(session_time), masking errors and potentially disabling timeout detection
crates/ralph/src/session/mod.rs:504-542 timeout-related tests currently derive timestamps from now_utc_rfc3339_or_fallback, increasing coupling to the fallback behavior
crates/ralph/src/queue.rs:290-301 backup_queue uses now_utc_rfc3339_or_fallback for backup filenames (fallback affects auditability)
crates/ralph/src/execution_history.rs:120-129 execution history entries persist timestamp from now_utc_rfc3339_or_fallback
callsite_count=50 via `rg -n now_utc_rfc3339_or_fallback\\( crates/ralph/src | wc -l`
plan: Audit all call sites of now_utc_rfc3339_or_fallback and identify which ones persist timestamps (vs doing comparisons)
Update now_utc_rfc3339_or_fallback to log the formatting error whenever the fallback path is taken (no silent fallback)
Change FALLBACK_RFC3339 to an unmistakable sentinel RFC3339 timestamp that still parses (e.g., 1970-01-01T00:00:00.000000000Z)
Refactor session timeout comparison to avoid parsing formatted now: compare against OffsetDateTime::now_utc() (or a test-injectable now) directly
Update/add unit tests for session timeout logic to use deterministic time inputs and add coverage for fallback usage semantics
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph` and `make ci`
notes: now_utc_rfc3339_or_fallback currently has 50 call sites, including persistence paths (queue backups, execution history, productivity stats); any sentinel/logging change should be validated against these IO/file-name uses.
Changed FALLBACK_RFC3339 from plausible 2026-01-18 to Unix epoch sentinel (1970-01-01)
Added error logging when fallback path is used
Refactored session validation to use OffsetDateTime directly, avoiding string roundtrip
Added comprehensive unit tests for timeout logic with deterministic time inputs
All 2221 tests pass; CI clean

tasks.title: TUI revert prompt should not silently default when reply channel closes
tasks.description:
Principles violated: Fail fast (F), Least astonishment (G), Operational hygiene (H).

The TUI's `revert_prompt` handler defaults to `RevertDecision::Keep` if the reply channel closes (`recv()` error). This silently hides an internal coordination failure between the runner thread and the UI event loop.

Why it matters (failure modes):
- If the UI thread is dropped/stuck and cannot answer, the runner proceeds with an implicit decision without surfacing that the prompt never completed.
- Defaulting to Keep can be surprising and may leave the repo in an unintended state in workflows where revert is expected on failure.

Minimal proposed fix:
- Replace `reply_rx.recv().unwrap_or(Keep)` with explicit error handling: log a warning/error and choose an explicit, documented default.
- Consider treating a closed reply channel as a hard error returned to the runner loop (safer) instead of silently selecting.
- Add a unit test that exercises the closed-channel path, asserting the chosen behavior.

Acceptance criteria:
- Tests cover the closed-channel path deterministically.
- Behavior is documented and consistent with the chosen safety default.
- `make ci` passes.
evidence: crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs:1776-1791 builds a RevertPromptHandler; if sending RunnerEvent::RevertPrompt fails it returns RevertDecision::Keep, and if reply_rx.recv() fails it defaults via unwrap_or(RevertDecision::Keep)
crates/ralph/src/tui/events/confirm.rs:195-241 stores the reply Sender in ConfirmRevertState/AppMode::ConfirmRevert, and UI completion is expected to send a RevertDecision back to the runner thread
crates/ralph/src/runutil/revert.rs:71-119 uses the RevertPromptHandler return value directly when git_revert_mode=ask (TUI prompt result drives apply_revert_decision)
Command: `nl -ba crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs | sed -n '1760,1810p'` and `nl -ba crates/ralph/src/tui/events/confirm.rs | sed -n '190,260p'`
plan: Decide and document the behavior for internal coordination failures in the TUI revert prompt (send-to-UI failure and reply channel close), including the safety default (Keep vs Revert vs abort/propagate error)
Implement explicit error handling for reply_rx.recv() (no silent unwrap_or) and ensure the send failure path is also surfaced (warning/status/log) rather than silently defaulting
Add a deterministic unit test covering the closed-channel reply path (and, if behavior differs, the send-to-UI failure path)
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes
notes: Revert prompt types/logic live in crates/ralph/src/runutil/revert.rs (re-exported via crates/ralph/src/runutil.rs); the TUI wires the handler in crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs.
Implemented fail-fast error handling for TUI revert prompt
Changed RevertPromptHandler to return Result<RevertDecision> instead of silently defaulting to Keep
Added 3 deterministic unit tests covering send failure, reply channel close, and success paths
Updated module documentation in revert.rs and revert_prompt.rs
All 2224+ tests pass; make ci completes successfully

tasks.title: Refactor duplicated config queue/id validation and fix resolve_id_width typo
tasks.description:
Principles violated: DRY (C), Consistency/Integrity (J), Make bugs hard to write (E).

Queue/id/path validation rules are duplicated between `validate_config` and the various `resolve_*` helpers, increasing drift risk and already producing an inconsistent error message typo.

Why it matters (failure modes):
- Divergent validation can lead to different behavior depending on code path (e.g., doctor vs normal resolve), and inconsistent user-facing errors.
- The current typo `Invalid_queue.id_width` reduces UX quality and makes error searchability harder.

Minimal proposed fix:
- Extract shared validators for queue id/path settings and call them from both `validate_config` and `resolve_*` helpers (single source of truth).
- Fix the typo in the `resolve_id_width` error message.
- Add unit tests that assert both `validate_config` and `resolve_*` return consistent errors for empty/invalid settings.

Acceptance criteria:
- Tests cover invalid id_prefix/id_width/file/done_file for both validation entrypoints.
- Error strings are consistent and typo-free.
- `make ci` passes.
evidence: crates/ralph/src/config.rs:125-136 calls validate_config(&cfg)? before resolve_id_prefix/resolve_id_width/resolve_queue_path/resolve_done_path
crates/ralph/src/config.rs:200-238 validates queue.id_prefix/id_width/file/done_file in validate_config
crates/ralph/src/config.rs:445-502 re-validates the same queue fields in resolve_* helpers
crates/ralph/src/config.rs:220 vs crates/ralph/src/config.rs:460 mismatch: 'Invalid queue.id_width' vs 'Invalid_queue.id_width'
crates/ralph/src/contracts/config.rs:91-111 defines QueueConfig fields (file/done_file/id_prefix/id_width) as the underlying data model

Command: `nl -ba crates/ralph/src/config.rs | sed -n '110,160p'` + `nl -ba crates/ralph/src/config.rs | sed -n '200,260p'` + `nl -ba crates/ralph/src/config.rs | sed -n '440,520p'` + `nl -ba crates/ralph/src/contracts/config.rs | sed -n '80,130p'`
plan: Introduce shared helper(s) for validating queue id/path fields (id_prefix/id_width/file/done_file), colocated with the config resolution logic or QueueConfig contracts
Update validate_config and resolve_* helpers to use the shared queue validation logic (single source of truth, consistent error strings)
Fix the resolve_id_width error message typo ('Invalid_queue.id_width' -> 'Invalid queue.id_width') and align any remaining queue validation error strings where feasible
Add unit tests that exercise both validate_config and resolve_* helpers for invalid explicit inputs (empty id_prefix, id_width=0, empty queue.file/done_file) and assert error text consistency
Verify with `cargo test -p ralph` and `make ci`
notes: As of 2026-02-07, resolve_from_cwd_internal already calls validate_config before resolve_*; the duplicated checks are currently defensive, but drift is visible via the id_width error string typo/inconsistency.

Refactored duplicated queue config validation into shared helpers (validate_queue_id_prefix_override, validate_queue_id_width_override, validate_queue_file_override, validate_queue_done_file_override)
Fixed typo: 'Invalid_queue.id_width' → 'Invalid queue.id_width'
Added 4 unit tests ensuring error consistency between validate_config and resolve_* helpers
All tests pass: cargo test -p ralph for new consistency tests

tasks.title: Centralize TaskPriority parsing to remove duplicated mappings
tasks.description:
Principles violated: DRY (C), Least astonishment (G), Consistency/Integrity (J).

`TaskPriority` parsing is implemented in multiple places with duplicated mappings and slightly different error strings. This creates drift risk and inconsistent user-facing errors across commands.

Why it matters (failure modes):
- Adding a new priority or changing accepted spellings requires editing multiple locations.
- Different commands can accept/reject different strings or produce inconsistent error messages.

Minimal proposed fix:
- Implement a single parser for `TaskPriority` (e.g., `impl FromStr for TaskPriority` in contracts, or a dedicated helper) and use it everywhere.
- Delete per-command `parse_priority` helpers in import/edit/preview.
- Keep/expand a single set of unit tests for case-insensitive parsing and consistent error messages.

Acceptance criteria:
- No duplicate `parse_priority*` helpers remain.
- Existing tests are updated and still pass; add missing tests for error text consistency.
- `make ci` passes.
evidence: crates/ralph/src/contracts/task.rs:131-189 defines TaskPriority + Display/as_str; no shared parser/FromStr exists
crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/import.rs:442-453 defines parse_priority (case-insensitive); error text uses "Expected: ..."
crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/edit.rs:351-362 defines parse_priority (duplicate mapping); error text uses "Expected one of: ..."
crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/edit.rs:652-663 defines parse_priority_for_preview (duplicate mapping + error text duplication)
Command: `rg -n "\\bparse_priority\\b|parse_priority_for_preview" crates/ralph/src -S`
plan: Implement a shared TaskPriority parser in `crates/ralph/src/contracts/task.rs` (prefer `impl std::str::FromStr for TaskPriority` with one canonical error message)
Refactor `crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/import.rs` and `crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/edit.rs` to use the shared parser (including preview/dry-run path)
Delete duplicate parse_priority helpers and centralize unit tests to cover: case-insensitive parsing; invalid-input error text; import/edit/preview consistency
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes
notes: TaskPriority is defined in `crates/ralph/src/contracts/task.rs` (not `crates/ralph/src/contracts/queue.rs`); scope/evidence updated to match current repo layout.
Centralized TaskPriority parsing with FromStr impl in contracts/task.rs
Removed duplicate parse_priority helpers from import.rs and edit.rs
All 2232+ tests pass; formatting and clippy clean
Error messages now consistent across all commands

tasks.title: Deduplicate TaskStatus parsing and cycling logic in queue edit preview
tasks.description:
Principles violated: DRY (C), Localize change (D), Consistency/Integrity (J).

`TaskStatus` parsing and status cycling are duplicated between the apply path and the preview (dry-run) path in `queue/operations/edit.rs`.

Why it matters (failure modes):
- Any future change to status order or accepted strings requires updating multiple helpers; drift would cause preview to disagree with real edits.
- Inconsistent error strings across preview vs apply increases UX confusion.

Minimal proposed fix:
- Reuse the same `cycle_status` and `parse_status` helpers for both apply and preview paths (or move them to a shared module used by both).
- Remove `*_for_preview` duplicates.
- Add a unit test asserting preview and apply paths interpret the same inputs and cycle order.

Acceptance criteria:
- No duplicate cycle_status_for_preview/parse_status_for_preview remain.
- Tests cover at least one cycle sequence and invalid status input for both paths.
- `make ci` passes.
evidence: crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/edit.rs:207-217 apply path uses cycle_status + parse_status for status edits
crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/edit.rs:446-456 preview path uses cycle_status_for_preview + parse_status_for_preview
crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/edit.rs:327-349 defines cycle_status/parse_status
crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/edit.rs:615-637 defines cycle_status_for_preview/parse_status_for_preview (duplicates)

Command: `nl -ba crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/edit.rs | sed -n '190,260p'`, `nl -ba crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/edit.rs | sed -n '430,520p'`, and `nl -ba crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/edit.rs | sed -n '600,660p'`
plan: Pick a single canonical status parser/cycle implementation (keep private helpers in edit.rs or move to TaskStatus in contracts/task.rs)
Refactor preview code paths to reuse the same cycle_status/parse_status logic as apply
Delete *_for_preview duplicates
Add unit tests in crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/edit.rs asserting preview_task_edit and apply_task_edit cycle the same status sequence and produce the same canonical invalid-status error string
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph` and `make ci`
notes: Related duplication: normalize_rfc3339_input_for_preview duplicates normalize_rfc3339_input in the same module; consider folding those together in a follow-up if not addressed in this change.
Removed duplicate cycle_status_for_preview/parse_status_for_preview functions
Refactored preview path to use canonical cycle_status/parse_status helpers
Added 2 unit tests verifying preview/apply parity for status cycling and error messages
All tests pass (2234+ unit tests, 2 new parity tests)
make ci passes completely

tasks.title: Fix AGENTS.md session ID format to match implementation and docs
tasks.description:
Principles violated: Consistency/Integrity (J), Least astonishment (G).

`AGENTS.md` and `docs/plugin-development.md` document a session ID format that does not match the actual implementation (`generate_phase_session_id`) or the main workflow docs (`docs/workflow.md`). This makes manual debugging and runner resumption error-prone.

Why it matters (failure modes):
- Contributors may pass the wrong `--session` values to runners when resuming/debugging sessions.
- Plugin authors may emit/expect the wrong `session_id` shape.
- Future maintainers can accidentally change code to match the wrong docs, creating backwards-incompatible behavior.

Minimal proposed fix:
- Update `AGENTS.md` to describe the actual format generated by `generate_phase_session_id`: `{task_id}-p{phase}-{timestamp}` (no `ralph-` prefix, no pid suffix).
- Update `docs/plugin-development.md` examples to use the same format.
- Confirm `docs/workflow.md` and code comments remain aligned.

Acceptance criteria:
- `AGENTS.md` session format and example match `docs/workflow.md` and `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/mod.rs`.
- `docs/plugin-development.md` no longer references the old `ralph-...-{pid}` format.
- `make ci` passes.
evidence: AGENTS.md:166-167 claims session IDs are `ralph-{task_id}-p{phase}-{timestamp}-{pid}` (example includes pid)
docs/plugin-development.md:218 shows `session_id` example as `ralph-RQ-0001-p2-1234567890-12345`
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/mod.rs:96-107 documents and generates `{task_id}-p{phase}-{timestamp}`
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/tests.rs:74-92 asserts generated IDs start with `{task_id}-p{phase}-`, do not start with `ralph-`, and have a digits-only timestamp suffix
docs/workflow.md:149-151 documents format `{task_id}-p{phase}-{timestamp}`
docs/configuration.md:96 documents format `{task_id}-p{phase}-{timestamp}`
docs/runner_cli_inventory.md:174-187 documents format `{task_id}-p{phase}-{timestamp}`
docs/kimi-continue-resume-session-trace.md:3-4 shows `--session <TASK_ID>-p<PHASE_NUMBER>-<CURRENT_TIME_SECS>` examples without `ralph-` prefix or pid suffix
Command: `rg -n "Session ID Format|generate_phase_session_id|--session|ralph-\{task_id\}" AGENTS.md docs/workflow.md docs/plugin-development.md crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/*`
plan: Update `AGENTS.md` session ID format and example to match `generate_phase_session_id` and `docs/workflow.md`
Update `docs/plugin-development.md` `session_id` example(s) to the same format
Repo-wide grep for `ralph-{task_id}` / pid-suffixed session IDs and update any remaining docs/examples
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes
notes: Also found an outdated `session_id` example in `docs/plugin-development.md` that still uses the `ralph-...-{pid}` format.
Fixed AGENTS.md session ID format from 'ralph-{task_id}-p{phase}-{timestamp}-{pid}' to '{task_id}-p{phase}-{timestamp}'
Fixed docs/plugin-development.md session_id example to match new format
All docs now consistent with implementation (generate_phase_session_id) and workflow docs

tasks.title: Split oversized TUI app module to reduce defect risk
tasks.description:
Principles violated: Localize change (D), KISS (A), Cohesion/SRP (D).

`crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs` is 2,271 lines (as of 2026-02-07), which increases navigation cost, makes reviews harder, and raises the risk of unrelated edits colliding.

Context:
- The TUI already has many extracted submodules (e.g., `app_filters`, `app_execution`, `app_logs`, `app_session`, `events`, `render`, `terminal`, etc.), but `app.rs` still contains the runtime/event loop (`run_tui`) and runner orchestration/threading (`RunnerEvent`) plus remaining TUI glue.

Why it matters (failure modes):
- Small UI/runtime changes still require touching a large, multi-responsibility file.
- Harder to add focused tests or reason about invariants; regressions are easier to introduce.

Minimal proposed fix:
- Further split `tui/app.rs` along remaining seams: runtime/runner (terminal setup + event loop + runner events) and any remaining state-mutation helpers that belong in existing `app_*` modules.
- Keep persistence/auto-save concerns in `crates/ralph/src/tui/app_session.rs`.
- Keep public TUI entrypoints stable (re-exports from `crates/ralph/src/tui/mod.rs`).
- Add module docs (`//!`) for any new files explaining responsibilities, non-responsibilities, and invariants.
- Keep refactor behavior-preserving; rely on existing test suite and add targeted unit tests for newly isolated logic.

Acceptance criteria:
- `crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs` reduced substantially (goal: <800 LOC) with extracted modules under `crates/ralph/src/tui/`.
- No behavior changes (tests pass).
- `make ci` passes.
evidence: Command: `wc -l crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs` => 2271
Command output: top TUI modules by LOC: `app.rs 2271`, `config_edit.rs 971`, `app_tasks.rs 840`, `app_parallel_state.rs 838` (from scanning `crates/ralph/src/tui/**/*.rs`)
Command: `rg -n "pub\\(crate\\) enum RunnerEvent" crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs` => line 1693
Command: `rg -n "pub fn run_tui" crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs` => line 1710
Pointer: `crates/ralph/src/tui/mod.rs` re-exports `run_tui` from `tui::app`: `pub use app::{App, prepare_tui_session, run_tui};`
plan: Audit `crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs` and map responsibility clusters to target modules, starting with existing splits (`app_session`, `terminal`, `events`, `render`, and the `app_*` modules)

Extract the runtime loop and runner orchestration (`RunnerEvent`, `run_tui`, spawn helpers, terminal setup/cleanup) into a dedicated module under `crates/ralph/src/tui/` while keeping the public exports stable via `crates/ralph/src/tui/mod.rs`
Ensure persistence/auto-save stays centralized in `crates/ralph/src/tui/app_session.rs` (avoid duplicating session logic during extraction)
Move remaining task/queue mutation helpers into `crates/ralph/src/tui/app_tasks.rs` (and related `app_*` modules) so `app.rs` becomes a thin facade around `App` state and entrypoints

Consolidate remaining cache/index/filter and execution/logging logic into the existing specialized modules (`app_filters`, `app_id_index`, `app_execution`, `app_logs`, `app_reload`) and delete any dead code left behind
Add focused tests near `crates/ralph/src/tui/tests/` for newly isolated logic and key invariants (log bounds, batch delete/archive side effects, runner state transitions, resize clamping)
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph`, then `make ci`, and confirm `crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs` is substantially reduced (goal: <800 LOC)
notes: As of 2026-02-07, `crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs` is still 2,271 LOC even though many TUI concerns are already split into `app_*`, `app_session`, `events/`, `render/`, and `terminal` modules.
Other near-oversize TUI modules exist (`config_edit.rs` ~971 LOC, `app_tasks.rs` ~840 LOC, `app_parallel_state.rs` ~838 LOC); follow-ups may be warranted, but this task focuses on `app.rs`.
Refactored tui/app.rs: extracted 766-line app_runtime.rs (runtime loop, runner orchestration) and 113-line app_resize.rs (resize/frame handling)
app.rs reduced from 2271 to 1522 lines (-749 lines); CI passes with all tests
Module docs present, public API stable via mod.rs re-exports, TerminalSession Drop guard ensures cleanup
5 new unit tests added for runner event handling

tasks.title: Split oversized run command module to improve maintainability
tasks.description:
Principles violated: Localize change (D), KISS (A), Cohesion/SRP (D).

`crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs` is still 1,734 lines (as of 2026-02-07). The run command has already been partially decomposed into submodules (`context.rs`, `iteration.rs`, `run_session.rs`, `selection.rs`, plus `phases/`, `parallel/`, `supervision/`), but `mod.rs` remains the central orchestration hub and is hard to evolve safely.

Why it matters (failure modes):
- Phase orchestration and resume/lock handling are tightly coupled (notably around `run_one_impl`), making changes risky and review difficult.
- Large file size + cross-cutting concerns make it harder to keep focused tests and module docs accurate (`mod.rs` currently self-reports a stale LOC estimate).

Minimal proposed fix:
- Continue the split by extracting additional cohesive units from `mod.rs` (e.g., run-one orchestration, run-loop orchestration, queue-lock/resume helpers), keeping the CLI API stable.
- Ensure each new module has `//!` docs and clear invariants; refresh/remove stale LOC claims.
- Keep refactor behavior-preserving; rely on existing unit + integration tests and `make ci`.

Acceptance criteria:
- `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs` reduced substantially (goal: <800 LOC) with extracted modules under `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/`.
- No behavior changes (existing tests pass).
- `make ci` passes.
evidence: Command: `wc -l crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs` => 1734 (2026-02-07)

Run command is already split into submodules, but `mod.rs` remains oversized: `ls crates/ralph/src/commands/run` shows `context.rs`, `iteration.rs`, `run_session.rs`, `selection.rs`, plus `phases/`, `parallel/`, `supervision/`

Behavior-preserving refactor safety net exists: unit tests in `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/tests.rs` (2617 LOC) and integration coverage like `crates/ralph/tests/stale_lock_cleanup_test.rs`

plan: Run baseline tests for run orchestration and resume/lock behavior (`cargo test -p ralph commands::run` and `cargo test -p ralph --test stale_lock_cleanup_test`)
Inventory the largest remaining blocks in `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs` (especially `run_one_impl`) and choose extraction boundaries that keep execution flow readable
Extract cohesive chunks into new submodules (e.g., `run_one.rs`, `run_loop.rs`, `queue_lock.rs`) and keep `mod.rs` as a thin facade with minimal re-exports
Refresh/adjust module docs (`//!`) for `mod.rs` and new modules; remove or update stale LOC claims and document invariants at the new boundaries
Move/adjust unit tests so they remain close to the code they validate (split `tests.rs` if it becomes the new hotspot) and ensure failure modes remain covered
Verify with `make ci` and fix any format/clippy/test fallout

tasks.title: Fix CONTRIBUTING.md CI gate step order and add drift guard
tasks.description:
Principles violated: Consistency/Integrity (J), Least astonishment (G).

`CONTRIBUTING.md` documents a CI gate step order that does not match the actual `make ci` pipeline. This is a concrete docs-code mismatch that can mislead contributors and cause churn when debugging CI failures locally.

Why it matters (failure modes):
- Contributors may run the wrong sequence when trying to reproduce failures or optimize local workflows.
- Docs drift is likely to recur unless guarded.

Minimal proposed fix:
- Update the documented pipeline order in `CONTRIBUTING.md` to match `Makefile:ci`.
- Add a small test (or extend an existing Makefile contract test) that asserts `CONTRIBUTING.md` contains the same ordered step list as the Makefile to prevent future drift.

Acceptance criteria:
- `CONTRIBUTING.md` CI step order matches Makefile `ci` recipe.
- New/updated test fails on drift and passes when aligned.
- `make ci` passes.
evidence: CONTRIBUTING.md:48-56 lists: check-env-safety → check-backup-artifacts → generate → format → type-check → lint → build → test → install (missing deps; misorders test/build/generate vs Makefile)
Makefile:125-139 runs: check-env-safety → check-backup-artifacts → deps → format → type-check → lint → test → build → generate → install
AGENTS.md:16 lists the Makefile order (already correct)
crates/ralph/tests/makefile_ci_contract_test.rs:129-159 only asserts relative ordering (format < type-check < lint); no drift guard for CONTRIBUTING.md

tasks.title: Fix config module docs precedence statement (defaults/global/project)
tasks.description:
Principles violated: Consistency/Integrity (J).

The module-level docs in `crates/ralph/src/config.rs` claim config merging precedence is `global → project → defaults`, which implies defaults override project config. This contradicts `docs/configuration.md` and typical layering (defaults first, then global, then project; CLI overrides both).

Why it matters:
- Misleading documentation causes incorrect future edits and misunderstandings when debugging config behavior.

Minimal proposed fix:
- Update the module docs line to reflect the real precedence order (defaults → global → project; CLI flags override both).

Acceptance criteria:
- `crates/ralph/src/config.rs` docs match `docs/configuration.md` and the actual resolution behavior.
- `make ci` passes.
evidence: crates/ralph/src/config.rs:23 says: `Config merging follows precedence: global → project → defaults.`
crates/ralph/src/config.rs:158-178 initializes `Config::default()` then applies global layer then project layer (project overrides global overrides defaults).
crates/ralph/src/contracts/config.rs:37-42 describes config layering (global defaults; project overrides) and leaf-wise merge semantics.
docs/configuration.md:372-376 lists precedence: CLI flags > project config > global config > schema defaults.
docs/competitive-analysis.md:165 summarizes config layers: CLI → Project → Global → Schema defaults.
Commands: `nl -ba crates/ralph/src/config.rs | sed -n '1,40p'`, `nl -ba crates/ralph/src/config.rs | sed -n '150,210p'`, `nl -ba docs/configuration.md | sed -n '360,390p'`
plan: Confirm effective precedence from code in `crates/ralph/src/config.rs` (`Config::default()` then apply global then apply project)
Update the precedence statement in `crates/ralph/src/config.rs` module docs to reflect defaults → global → project (project overrides global overrides defaults; CLI overrides handled elsewhere)
Search for other config-layer precedence statements (e.g., `docs/configuration.md`, `docs/cli.md`, `docs/competitive-analysis.md`) and ensure wording is consistent
Run `make ci` and confirm it passes
notes: As of 2026-02-07, config resolution applies layers in order: defaults (`Config::default()`), then global config, then project config; the module doc precedence line is stale.
Fixed config precedence docs: changed line 23 from 'global → project → defaults' to 'defaults, then global, then project' to match actual implementation behavior
Verified against docs/configuration.md (lines 372-376) and contracts/config.rs (lines 37-42)
make ci passes (all 2237 tests)

tasks.title: Implement dependency-based auto-execution for unblocked tasks
tasks.description:
Ralph already enforces `depends_on` for runnability and can explain blockers via `ralph queue explain`. However, there is no wait/poll mode for dependency unblocking: if all remaining tasks are blocked by dependencies (or schedule), `ralph run loop` exits and users must rerun after queue/done changes. The TUI also lacks an at-a-glance readiness summary (ready vs blocked-by-deps vs blocked-by-schedule).

Goals:
1. Add `ralph run loop --wait-when-blocked` (name TBD) to poll `.ralph/queue.json`/`.ralph/done.json` until a runnable task appears, with optional timeout.
2. Optionally notify when tasks become runnable (reuse existing notify/webhook plumbing).
3. Add TUI indicator summarizing readiness counts.

Non-goals:
- Implement dependency graph visualization (already exists via `ralph queue graph` and the TUI dependency graph overlay).
- Change task statuses when dependencies are met (blocked is a runnability condition, not a status).
evidence: Current behavior: `depends_on` blocks runnable selection via `are_dependencies_met`/`is_task_runnable` (crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/query.rs).
Current capability: `ralph queue explain` and `ralph run --dry-run` report unmet dependencies and schedules via runnability reports (crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/runnability.rs).

Current capability: dependency visualization already exists via `ralph queue graph` and the TUI dependency graph overlay (crates/ralph/src/queue/graph, crates/ralph/src/tui/dependency_graph_cache.rs).

Gap observed: CLI run loop exits when no runnable task exists (e.g., all todo candidates are blocked by deps/schedule); there is no wait/poll mode to continue once unblocked (crates/ralph/src/commands/run/run_loop.rs).

Gap observed: TUI list/details show dependency IDs, but there is no readiness summary or per-task 'deps satisfied' indicator in the list (crates/ralph/src/tui/render/panels/list.rs, crates/ralph/src/tui/render/panels/details.rs).
plan: Define CLI contract for waiting when the queue has todo candidates but none runnable (e.g., add `ralph run loop --wait-when-blocked`, plus `--wait-timeout-seconds` and `--wait-poll-ms`).
Wire new flags through CLI arg parsing and into `commands::run::RunLoopOptions` (crates/ralph/src/cli/run.rs, crates/ralph/src/commands/run/run_loop.rs).

Implement a wait loop: when `run_one` returns `RunOutcome::NoTodo`, compute a runnability report; if tasks are blocked-by-deps/schedule and wait mode is enabled, poll `.ralph/queue.json`/`.ralph/done.json` until a runnable task appears, timeout is reached, or stop signal is set.
Reuse existing notification/webhook plumbing to optionally notify when runnable tasks appear (and include a concise readiness summary).
Add tests for wait-mode behavior: immediate exit when runnable exists; wait then continue when queue/done changes; respects timeout and stop signal.
TUI: compute and render readiness summary counts (ready, blocked_by_dependencies, blocked_by_schedule) in header/footer.
TUI: optionally add a lightweight per-task marker for unmet dependencies in the list panel (avoid expensive recomputation per draw).
Docs: update `docs/cli.md` with the new flags and examples; add a short dependency/unblocking note to `docs/workflow.md` (with links to `docs/queue-and-tasks.md`).
notes: Repo reality check: dependency logic is implemented in queue validation + queue operations (no crates/ralph/src/queue/dependencies.rs).
Dependency visualization already exists (CLI `queue graph`, TUI dependency graph overlay); remaining gap is wait/notification + readiness surfacing.
Implemented dependency-based auto-execution for unblocked tasks
Added --wait-when-blocked, --wait-poll-ms, --wait-timeout-seconds, --notify-when-unblocked flags
Added QueueUnblocked webhook event and desktop notifications
Updated docs/cli.md and docs/workflow.md with usage examples
All 2237 tests pass, CI gate successful

tasks.title: Add configuration profiles for quick workflow switching
tasks.description:
No way to quickly switch between different workflow configurations. Users must manually edit config or pass many CLI flags. Add named profiles that bundle runner, model, phases, and other settings.

Goals:
1. Add `profiles` section to config with named presets
2. Support `ralph run --profile quick-fix` or `ralph scan --profile thorough`
3. Profiles override base config but CLI flags override profiles
4. Include built-in profiles: quick (phases=1, runner=kimi), thorough (phases=3, runner=claude)
5. Add `ralph config profiles` command to list/manage profiles

Evidence:
- Current: Config has agent.* settings but no grouping mechanism
- Gap: No quick way to switch between 'quick fix' vs 'deep review' modes
- User pain: Must remember and type multiple flags: `--runner claude --model opus --phases 3 --effort high`
evidence: Config resolution loads defaults -> global config (~/.config/ralph/config.json or $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/ralph/config.json) -> project config (.ralph/config.json) via config::resolve_from_cwd/apply_layer in crates/ralph/src/config.rs; there is no profile selection layer today

contracts::Config has agent.* and leaf-wise merge semantics (AgentConfig.merge_from, optional agent.phase_overrides), but no config-level profiles map exists in crates/ralph/src/contracts/config.rs (and schemas/config.schema.json contains no \"profiles\" property)
CLI agent flags in crates/ralph/src/agent/args.rs cover runner/model/effort/phases/quick/repo-prompt/runner_cli/etc, but there is no --profile flag

Run/scan handlers resolve config then compute CLI overrides via agent::resolve_run_agent_overrides / agent::resolve_agent_overrides in crates/ralph/src/agent/resolve.rs; there is no step that applies a named preset between config defaults and CLI/task overrides

docs/configuration.md documents agent.* and per-phase precedence but does not describe configuration profiles; docs/cli.md uses the word \"profile\" only in unrelated contexts (shell profile install text, PRD example)
plan: Extend the config contract: add a profiles map (e.g., BTreeMap<String, AgentConfig>-shaped overrides) to contracts::Config in crates/ralph/src/contracts/config.rs and define explicit merge semantics (union keys; when a name collides, merge leaf-wise using AgentConfig.merge_from)

Extend config parsing and layering in crates/ralph/src/config.rs: add profiles to ConfigLayer, and merge profiles during apply_layer with correct precedence (defaults < global < project; project profile values override global for the same profile name)

Add `--profile <NAME>` to CLI: add a profile field to crates/ralph/src/agent/args.rs (AgentArgs + RunAgentArgs), and add the flag to crates/ralph/src/cli/scan.rs (ScanArgs does not flatten AgentArgs); plumb the value through crates/ralph/src/cli/run.rs, crates/ralph/src/cli/scan.rs, and the relevant task commands that accept agent args

Implement profile application: resolve base config (defaults/global/project) -> apply selected profile onto resolved.config.agent (and any nested agent.* such as phase overrides) -> apply existing precedence rules for task.agent and CLI flags (explicitly document final precedence as: CLI flags > task.agent > selected profile > base config)

Add built-in profiles (fallback when not defined in config): quick (runner=kimi, model=kimi-for-coding, phases=1) and thorough (runner=claude, model=opus, phases=3); include built-ins in list output and unknown-name validation errors
Add `ralph config profiles` subcommands in crates/ralph/src/cli/config.rs (at least list + show), with useful --help examples and clear output for built-in vs user-defined profiles

Update help text and docs: update crates/ralph/src/cli/run.rs and crates/ralph/src/cli/scan.rs long help to include --profile examples and precedence; update docs/configuration.md and docs/cli.md with config examples and the override order
Regenerate and commit schemas/config.schema.json via `make generate` and verify schema includes profiles

Add tests: config profile merge + application precedence in crates/ralph/src/config.rs tests; clap parsing/help coverage for --profile in crates/ralph/src/cli/run.rs and crates/ralph/src/cli/scan.rs tests; unknown profile name errors list available profiles (including built-ins)
notes: Scope refresh: crates/ralph/src/config/loader.rs does not exist; config resolution lives in crates/ralph/src/config.rs
As of 2026-02-07, there is no config-level profiles feature and no `--profile` flag in CLI; the only "profile" mentions in docs are unrelated (shell profile install text, PRD example)
Added profiles field to Config contract (BTreeMap<String, AgentConfig>)
Added --profile flag to ralph run and ralph scan commands
Implemented built-in profiles: quick (kimi/1-phase) and thorough (claude/opus/3-phase)
Added ralph config profiles list/show subcommands
Profile propagation to parallel workers implemented
Updated docs/configuration.md and docs/cli.md with profile documentation
Regenerated schemas/config.schema.json with profiles support
All 2252+ tests passing, CI gate passes
