tasks.title: Add intelligent retry with exponential backoff for transient failures
tasks.description:
Runner failures are currently terminal with no automatic retry. Network issues, rate limits, or temporary runner unavailability require manual intervention. Add smart retry classification and exponential backoff.

Goals:
1. Classify failures as retryable vs non-retryable vs requires-user-input
2. Add exponential backoff (with cap + jitter) for retryable failures (network, rate limit, temporary unavailability)
3. Add agent config for runner retry (defaults: 3 attempts, 1000ms base backoff). Avoid colliding with existing webhook retry settings.
4. Distinguish runner-specific failures (e.g., rate limit vs auth failure)
5. Surface retry attempt count and next-backoff delay in the TUI execution view

Evidence:
- Current: RunnerError enum has variants but no retry classification
- Gap: Runner execution does not auto-retry transient failures
- User pain: Transient failures require manual restart
evidence: Runner execution errors are handled in crates/ralph/src/runutil/execution.rs; RunnerError::Timeout and RunnerError::Interrupted abort the phase (no automatic retry/backoff loop).

For RunnerError::NonZeroExit and RunnerError::TerminatedBySignal, there is a manual Continue/resume path after git revert, but it is not an automatic retry mechanism and requires a captured session_id.
Runner execution is centralized through crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/process.rs and plugin dispatch (crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/plugin_executor.rs) with no retry classification layer.

Retry/backoff already exists for other subsystems (webhook delivery retry_count/retry_backoff_ms; lock cleanup backoff), so runner retry config should use distinct keys and align semantics where possible.
TUI currently does not show retry attempt/backoff status during runner execution.
plan: Define agent-level runner retry config in crates/ralph/src/contracts/config.rs (defaults: 3 attempts, 1000ms base; include multiplier, max_backoff, jitter). Use distinct names to avoid WebhookConfig retry_count/retry_backoff_ms collisions.

Add a centralized retry decision helper (e.g., RetryDecision/FailureClassification) that maps (runner, RunnerError, exit code, stderr/stdout hints) to { retryable, requires_user_input, non_retryable } plus a backoff duration.
Implement the retry loop at the runner invocation layer (crates/ralph/src/runutil/execution.rs) so it applies consistently across phases and runners; ensure Ctrl-C/stop signals still abort promptly.

Start conservative: retry only clearly transient classes (Timeout, select Io, and explicit rate-limit patterns). Do not retry BinaryMissing/auth failures; ensure errors that should surface immediately still do.

Plumb an in-memory retry attempt counter and next-backoff delay to TUI execution rendering (crates/ralph/src/tui/app_execution.rs); avoid persisting attempt counts into queue unless there is a concrete reporting requirement.

Add tests: backoff calculation (cap + jitter), classification matrix (retryable vs not), and end-to-end runner backend simulation (fail N times then succeed) without sleeping (injectable clock/sleeper).
Update docs (docs/error-handling.md, docs/configuration.md) and regenerate schema (schemas/config.schema.json via make generate).
Verify locally with make ci and at least one simulated transient runner failure (timeout or injected non-zero exit with rate-limit signature).
notes: Config already includes webhook retry_count/retry_backoff_ms; runner retry settings should use distinct agent keys to avoid confusion.
There are existing retry/backoff precedents (CI gate auto-retry; lock cleanup backoff) that can guide runner retry behavior and defaults.
Implemented intelligent retry with exponential backoff for transient failures
Added RunnerRetryConfig, failure classification (RunnerFailureClass), and retry loop in execution.rs
Fixed Clippy warnings and updated documentation
All 11 retry-specific tests pass; 2254 total tests pass

custom_fields.user_value: Reduces manual intervention for transient failures, improves success rate, handles network hiccups

tasks.title: Implement continuous execution mode with background daemon capability
tasks.description:
No way to run Ralph as a persistent background service. `ralph watch` is foreground-only, and `ralph run loop` exits when there are no runnable candidates. Add a continuous loop mode that stays alive, monitors `.ralph/queue.json`, and auto-executes new tasks as they appear.

Goals:
1. Add `ralph run loop --wait-when-empty` (alias `--continuous`) that waits indefinitely when the queue is empty
2. Support configurable idle poll interval while empty (default 30s)
3. Add queue-change trigger (wake immediately when `.ralph/queue.json` or `.ralph/done.json` changes; poll fallback)
4. Reuse existing graceful stop mechanism (`ralph queue stop` and Ctrl+C)
5. Add `ralph daemon start|stop|status` as a wrapper around the continuous loop (initial Unix focus; document systemd/launchd templates)

User pain:
- Must keep a terminal open or rely on external process managers (screen, systemd, launchd)
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/run_loop.rs` exits when `run_one` returns `RunOutcome::NoCandidates` ("RunLoop: end (no more todo tasks remaining)")
`crates/ralph/src/commands/run/run_loop.rs` returns early when there are zero todo tasks at startup ("No todo tasks found.")

`--wait-when-blocked` exists (`crates/ralph/src/cli/run.rs`) and polls `.ralph/queue.json` + `.ralph/done.json`, but the wait loop currently returns `NoCandidates` when the queue becomes empty (no "wait when empty" mode)
Graceful stop already exists via `ralph queue stop`, which creates `.ralph/cache/stop_requested` (`crates/ralph/src/signal.rs`) and is honored by the run loop
Queue lock is directory-based at `.ralph/lock` and records PID/command/label (`crates/ralph/src/lock.rs`), which can serve as a single-instance guard for a daemon
No `ralph daemon` command/module exists today (there is no `crates/ralph/src/commands/daemon.rs`)
plan: Add `--wait-when-empty` (alias `--continuous`) and `--empty-poll-ms` (default: 30000) to `ralph run loop` (`crates/ralph/src/cli/run.rs`); document constraints (sequential-only; conflicts with `--parallel` and `--interactive`)

Extend `RunLoopOptions` and `run_loop` (`crates/ralph/src/commands/run/run_loop.rs`) to wait instead of exiting on `RunOutcome::NoCandidates` when enabled; reuse existing stop signal + Ctrl+C semantics
Refactor the waiting logic (`wait_for_runnable_task`) to support both blocked-wait and empty-wait without duplicating polling/stop handling; keep existing `--wait-when-blocked` behavior unchanged
Add optional filesystem wakeups using `notify` to watch `.ralph/queue.json` and `.ralph/done.json` (reuse patterns from `crates/ralph/src/commands/watch/`); fall back to polling for portability

Implement `ralph daemon start|stop|status` as a thin wrapper around `ralph run loop --wait-when-empty` (initial Unix implementation): persist state to `.ralph/cache/daemon.json`; enforce single-instance via `.ralph/lock` ownership metadata (`crates/ralph/src/lock.rs`)
Document recommended service setups (systemd/launchd) and daemon usage in `docs/cli.md` and `docs/workflow.md`
Add integration tests for continuous mode: loop stays alive on empty queue, wakes on queue change, exits on stop signal (`ralph queue stop`)
Add integration tests for daemon wrapper (Unix-only if needed): start -> status reports running -> stop shuts down cleanly; verify no leftover lock/stop signal
notes: Current repo already supports waiting when blocked (`--wait-when-blocked`) and graceful stop (`ralph queue stop`); the missing piece is waiting when the queue is empty and a first-class background runner wrapper.
Implemented --wait-when-empty (alias --continuous) flag for ralph run loop
Added configurable empty-poll-ms with 30s default and 50ms minimum
Implemented filesystem notifications (notify crate) with poll fallback for queue changes
Added ralph daemon start|stop|status commands with Unix process management
Added comprehensive documentation and integration tests

tasks.title: Stabilize make ci: fix flaky run_one_with_id_locked runnable-status assertion
tasks.description:
Principles violated: H (Operational hygiene), F (Fail fast), J (Consistency/Integrity).

A maintenance scan flagged an intermittent `make ci` failure caused by a brittle string assertion in a core unit test. In current repo state (2026-02-07), the flake was not reproducible (30 consecutive runs passed, and `make ci` completed successfully), but the underlying issue remains: tests are asserting on `anyhow::Error::to_string()` substrings, which can change with context chaining or display formatting.

Evidence:
- `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/tests.rs:312` checks `message.contains("is not runnable")` on `err.to_string()`.
- `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/selection.rs:195,203` assert `err.to_string().contains("not runnable")` for Done/Rejected targets.
- `crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/query.rs:181` emits the user-facing "... is not runnable ..." message for Done/Rejected target tasks.
- `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/queue_lock.rs:57` provides `is_queue_lock_already_held_error`, a stable helper for detecting lock contention without substring matching.

Why it matters:
- Small changes in error context/display can break tests and reintroduce CI flakes.
- These tests should assert stable invariants (typed error, or a stable sentinel/constant) rather than `anyhow` display strings.

Minimal proposed fix:
- Prefer a typed/domain error for runnability/selection; otherwise introduce a single-source constant/sentinel token in the production error path and assert on that.
- Refactor affected tests to assert the stable invariant and use `is_queue_lock_already_held_error` for lock detection.

Acceptance criteria:
- `make ci` passes, and running it twice in a row passes.
- `cargo test -p ralph` passes without intermittent failures.
- The test asserts on a stable invariant (typed error or stable code/constant), not a brittle `anyhow` display string.
evidence: File: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/tests.rs:312 asserts on `anyhow::Error::to_string()` via `message.contains(\"is not runnable\")`.
File: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/selection.rs:195 and :203 assert on `err.to_string().contains(\"not runnable\")` for Done/Rejected targets.
File: crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/query.rs:181 emits the user-facing "... is not runnable ..." error for Done/Rejected target tasks.
File: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/queue_lock.rs:57 provides `is_queue_lock_already_held_error`, a stable helper for detecting queue-lock contention without substring matching.
Observed 2026-02-07: `cargo test -p ralph commands::run::tests::run_one_with_id_locked_skips_reacquiring_queue_lock -- --exact` passed 30 consecutive iterations (flake not reproduced).
Observed 2026-02-07: `make ci` completed successfully.
plan: Confirm the intended invariant for `commands::run::tests::run_one_with_id_locked_skips_reacquiring_queue_lock`: the error should be a runnable-status failure (Done/Rejected) and must not be a queue-lock error.

Inventory all runnability-related tests that assert on `anyhow::Error::to_string()` (use `rg -n -F "to_string().contains(" crates/ralph/src/commands/run crates/ralph/src/queue` and `rg -n "not runnable" crates/ralph/src`).
Pick a stable assertion surface: prefer a typed/domain error; otherwise introduce a single-source constant/sentinel token in the production error path and assert on that token.

Refactor `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/tests.rs` and `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/selection.rs` to assert on the stable invariant rather than `anyhow` display strings; use `is_queue_lock_already_held_error` for lock contention detection.
Add/adjust deterministic unit tests covering Done/Rejected target selection failure and ensuring lock contention is not the failure mode.
Verify by running `cargo test -p ralph` and then `make ci` twice consecutively.
notes: 2026-02-07: reran `cargo test -p ralph commands::run::tests::run_one_with_id_locked_skips_reacquiring_queue_lock -- --exact` 30x; could not reproduce the flake, and `make ci` completed successfully. Assertions remain string-based in `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/tests.rs` and `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/selection.rs`; a stable lock detector exists at `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/queue_lock.rs:57`.

tasks.title: Implement multi-tab and multi-window workspace support with project isolation
tasks.description: Add comprehensive multi-tab and multi-window support to the Ralph Mac app, enabling users to work on multiple projects simultaneously with complete isolation between workspaces. Users can open multiple tabs within a single window (default behavior) and drag tabs out to create independent windows. Each workspace maintains its own working directory, queue state, recent directories, and CLI client instance - projects never comingle. Implement native macOS tab bar (NSTabViewController integration) with Safari-style tab appearance, drag-to-reorder, and tear-off support. Include keyboard shortcuts (Cmd+T new tab, Cmd+W close tab/window, Cmd+Shift+] tab navigation), window restoration on app relaunch, and visual indicators showing active project paths in tab titles. This transforms Ralph from a single-project tool into a true multi-project workspace manager.

tasks.title: Eliminate Rust and Swift test flakiness in local CI (make macos-ci)
tasks.description: Stabilize the mandatory local CI gate by removing nondeterminism and flaky failures across Rust and Swift test suites while preserving full test coverage. Improve execution speed only where it does not increase flakiness, and verify deterministic behavior through repeated back-to-back runs.
evidence: Makefile test target runs two Rust suites with --include-ignored and locked deps, creating isolated temp dirs via TMPDIR/TEMP/TMP under target/tmp/ralph-ci-tmp/.
Makefile macos-ci target runs ci, then macos-build, then macos-test, so Rust and Swift reliability issues both impact the ship gate.
The shared RalphMac scheme executes RalphCoreTests only; Swift coverage lives in apps/RalphMac/RalphCoreTests (3 test files, 14 tests total).
RalphE2ESmokeTests falls back to building ralph with cargo when no binary is present (RALPH_BIN_PATH env var controls this, adds toolchain-dependent variability).
crates/ralph/tests/daemon_integration_test.rs explicitly avoids full daemon lifecycle coverage due to flaky subprocess behavior (126 lines, Unix-gated).
CI auto-retry behavior exists in crates/ralph/src/commands/run/supervision/ci.rs with CI_GATE_AUTO_RETRY_LIMIT=5 for runner phases, but local make ci/macos-ci still requires deterministic tests.
crates/ralph/tests/test_support.rs (606 lines) provides shared test helpers including temp_dir_outside_repo() for isolated test directories.
crates/ralph/tests/test_support_integration_test.rs (97 lines) tests the test helpers themselves.
crates/ralph/tests/git_repo_ci_gate_custom_test.rs (112 lines) has active tests for custom CI gate configuration.
No explicit retry logic exists in Makefile targets; uses set -euo pipefail for fail-fast behavior.
Temp directory cleanup controlled by RALPH_CI_KEEP_TMP env var; preserves dirs when set to 1 for debugging.
plan: Capture a reproducible baseline by looping make test and make macos-test back-to-back, recording per-target failures, duration, and rerun variance.

Triage failures by nondeterminism source (shared filesystem state, clock/timing assumptions, subprocess lifecycle, environment/toolchain dependency) and map each source to concrete Rust or Swift test files.
Harden Rust tests first by enforcing deterministic setup/teardown around temp paths, timestamps, and process state using existing test_support helpers where possible.
Harden Swift tests next by removing timing-sensitive assertions and stabilizing CLI binary resolution so tests do not depend on opportunistic cargo builds during execution.
Align CI entrypoints so make test, make ci, make macos-test, and make macos-ci run with consistent prerequisites and deterministic environment settings.
Add or tighten tests near touched code to cover both success paths and previously flaky failure modes without reducing test coverage.
Prove stability with repeated stress runs of Rust and Swift suites plus full make ci/macos-ci, and only close when consecutive runs produce zero flakes.
notes: Scope expanded to include test_support_integration_test.rs discovered during analysis.
Added relates_to: RQ-0717 (completed task about stabilizing make ci) for historical context.
Swift tests use async/await patterns with TaskGroups; E2E tests may have race conditions in timeout handling.
Consider using RALPH_CI_KEEP_TMP=1 for debugging temp directory issues during test development.
Eliminated test flakiness by removing opportunistic cargo builds
Added adaptive polling with exponential backoff to wait_until
Increased timeouts across timing-sensitive tests
Aligned CI entrypoints to use pre-built release binary
tasks.request: The Rust tests are extremely time consuming and flaky. I need these to work better. Focus on ensuring no more flakiness. I don't want to forgo running all tests but if there is a way to speed them up without increasing flakiness please do that too. Overall flakiness is absurd. Back to back to back runs can produce different failures. Similar issues for the swift app tests. Overall, all test flakiness needs eliminated so our mandatory local CI gate is more reliable. Currently it flakes and causes wasted time.

tasks.title: Fix @unchecked Sendable data race risks in core classes
tasks.description: Workspace, RalphCLIRun, and QueueFileWatcher use @unchecked Sendable to suppress Swift concurrency warnings. This is unsafe as these classes have mutable shared state that could cause data races. Proper concurrency isolation with @MainActor or actors should be implemented.
evidence: Workspace.swift line 24: 'public final class Workspace: ObservableObject, Identifiable, Codable, @unchecked Sendable'
RalphCLIClient.swift line 67: 'public final class RalphCLIRun: @unchecked Sendable' (uses NSLock for partial protection)
QueueFileWatcher.swift line 23: 'public final class QueueFileWatcher: @unchecked Sendable' (FSEvents bridge)
GraphModels.swift line 106: 'extension CGPoint: @unchecked Sendable {}' (minor: CGPoint value type extension)
Workspace.swift: 35+ @Published properties accessed across thread boundaries
RalphCLIRun: 6 of 8 mutable properties lack synchronization (only exitStatus/exitWaiters use NSLock)
Project already uses SWIFT_STRICT_CONCURRENCY=complete and Swift 6.2

tasks.title: Fix kimi runner stream-json output parsing to capture all outputs
tasks.description: Investigate and fix the kimi runner's stream-json output parsing to ensure all outputs are captured and displayed correctly. Currently, Ralph's parser is missing some outputs when using the kimi runner compared to running kimi directly. The kimi CLI uses a specific JSON format with TextPart, ThinkPart, ToolCall, and ToolResult events that get assembled into Message objects with role='assistant' or role='tool'. The parser needs to handle all these event types properly, including thinking/reasoning content (type='think'), tool call arguments, and tool results. The goal is for the output seen when using kimi through Ralph to match identically to what users see when running kimi directly.

tasks.title: Fix ralph queue next-id to work with duplicate IDs
tasks.description: The "ralph queue next-id" command currently fails when the queue has duplicate task IDs because it calls load_and_validate_queues() which enforces ID uniqueness. This is problematic because users need this command precisely when they have duplicate IDs and want to find the next available ID to fix the collision. tasks.title: Fix race condition in RetryHelperTests.test_delayCalculation_withBackoff
tasks.description: The test test_delayCalculation_withBackoff fails with 'Index out of range' when accessing delays[0]. The root cause is a race condition in the onProgress callback, which is currently synchronous, forcing callers to spawn detached tasks (using `Task { ... }`) for async operations like recording delays. These detached tasks may not complete before the execute() method returns and the test reads the results. The fix is to make RetryProgressHandler async and await it in RetryHelper.
evidence: Test failure: '💣 Swift runtime failure: Index out of range' at RetryHelperTests.swift:287
RetryHelperTests.swift (lines 141, 279, 311) uses `Task { await tracker.record(...) }` in onProgress callbacks
RetryHelper.swift (line 87) defines RetryProgressHandler as a synchronous closure, preventing direct await in progress handlers
The race condition occurs because execute() returns immediately after the last attempt failure, before detached tasks in onProgress can finish
Detached tasks are particularly problematic with small base delays (0.01s) used in tests
plan: Modify RetryHelper.swift: Change RetryProgressHandler typealias to be async
Modify RetryHelper.swift: Add await to all onProgress?() calls in execute and executeWithResult
Modify RetryHelperTests.swift: Remove Task { ... } from onProgress handlers in test_progressCallbackCalledOnRetry, test_delayCalculation_withBackoff, and test_delayCalculation_respectsMaxDelay
Modify Workspace.swift: Update any synchronous onProgress handlers to match the new async signature
Verify all RetryHelperTests pass consistently without race conditions
Run make macos-ci to verify full CI gate passes
notes: Making the progress handler async is the root cause fix as it allows deterministic synchronization between the operation and progress tracking.
The 15-second CI hold-up mentioned in the request may be compounded by the test suite execution time and the frequent retries during failed test scenarios.
Synchronized RetryProgressHandler by making it async and awaiting it in RetryHelper.

tasks.title: Fix `macos-ci` target ignoring failures from build and test steps
tasks.description: The `macos-ci` target in the `Makefile` improperly chains sub-targets using `; \` within a single shell block without `set -e`. This allows subsequent commands (including the final success echo) to execute even if `macos-build` or `macos-test` fails with a non-zero exit code. In the reported logs, `xcodebuild` failed with `Error 65`, yet the CI gate reported `✓ macOS CI completed` and the task was marked as done. This target must be refactored to either use proper `make` dependencies or ensure that any failure immediately halts execution and returns an error.

tasks.title: Fix session resume to handle dirty repo check before retry loop
tasks.description:
When resuming an incomplete session via `ralph run loop`, the system gets stuck in an infinite retry loop if the repo is dirty. The run loop detects the incomplete session, prompts the user to resume, but then immediately fails with 'repo is dirty' error. Instead of stopping gracefully or prompting to clean the repo, it retries the same task 50 times (the consecutive failure limit) with the same error each time before finally aborting.

The session resume logic should:
1. Check repo cleanliness BEFORE attempting to resume a session, OR
2. Treat 'repo is dirty' as a non-retryable error that immediately stops the loop with a clear message, OR
3. Re-prompt the user to clean the repo after the first failure

Currently, the loop wastes 50 retry attempts on an error that cannot self-resolve, providing poor UX and delaying the actual fix (cleaning the repo).
evidence: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/run_loop.rs:87-127: Session resume detection happens BEFORE repo cleanliness check

crates/ralph/src/commands/run/run_one/orchestration.rs:293-301: Repo cleanliness check happens AFTER session validation (lines 139-152)
crates/ralph/src/git/clean.rs:45-105: require_clean_repo_ignoring_paths() returns GitError::DirtyRepo when repo has disallowed changes
crates/ralph/src/git/error.rs:23-24: GitError::DirtyRepo variant with message 'repo is dirty; commit/stash your changes before running Ralph'
MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES = 50 in run_loop.rs causes 50 retries before abort
The run loop's abort_reason() in runutil/abort.rs does not recognize dirty repo errors as abort-worthy
Session ID format: {task_id}-p{phase}-{timestamp} (e.g., RQ-0446-p1-1704153600)

tasks.title: Merge update_task_before_run functionality into Phase 1 planning
tasks.description: Merge the task updater functionality currently enabled via the 'update_task_before_run' config key directly into the Phase 1 (planning) phase. This functionality updates task fields (scope, evidence, plan, notes, tags, depends_on) from the current repository state before execution. The separate config parameter should be removed as this becomes default behavior integrated into Phase 1. Also remove the related 'fail_on_prerun_update_error' config key and the --update-task/--no-update-task CLI flags.
evidence: User request: Merge the task updater functionality enabled via the config key 'update_task_before_run' into the Phase 1 plan phase. It does not make sense to have this run separately. The config parameter can go away as this is enabled by default.

Current implementation still performs optional pre-run task update in crates/ralph/src/commands/run/run_one/orchestration.rs (around lines 297-392), including warn-or-fail behavior via fail_on_prerun_update_error.
Phase 1 planning entrypoint exists in crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/phase1.rs (execute_phase1_planning), and currently has no update_task_before_run wiring.
AgentConfig still defines update_task_before_run and fail_on_prerun_update_error in crates/ralph/src/contracts/config.rs, with merge/default/test coverage in the same file.
CLI run flags and help text still expose --update-task/--no-update-task in crates/ralph/src/cli/run.rs.
Override propagation still maps update-task behavior in crates/ralph/src/agent/resolve.rs and crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/args.rs.
Docs and generated schema still document these knobs in docs/configuration.md, docs/features/configuration.md, docs/cli.md, crates/ralph/assets/ralph_readme.md, and schemas/config.schema.json.
Sanity checks and tests still reference the keys in crates/ralph/src/sanity/mod.rs, crates/ralph/tests/config_test.rs, and crates/ralph/src/commands/run/tests.rs.
plan: Move pre-run task update orchestration from crates/ralph/src/commands/run/run_one/orchestration.rs into Phase 1 flow so it executes once before planning prompt generation.
Wire the updater through Phase 1 interfaces in crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/phase1.rs and crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/mod.rs without re-introducing a separate pre-run toggle.
Remove update_task_before_run and fail_on_prerun_update_error from AgentConfig in crates/ralph/src/contracts/config.rs, including merge/default handling and contract tests.

Remove run-level CLI toggles --update-task/--no-update-task in crates/ralph/src/cli/run.rs and delete override plumbing in crates/ralph/src/agent/resolve.rs and crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/args.rs.
Update task update integration points in crates/ralph/src/commands/task/update.rs or callers as needed so behavior remains deterministic when Phase 1 owns task refresh.
Update guardrails/sanity references in crates/ralph/src/sanity/mod.rs and any code paths still expecting the removed config keys.
Update impacted tests in crates/ralph/tests/config_test.rs and crates/ralph/src/commands/run/tests.rs (plus nearby unit tests) for the new default behavior.
Add migration handling for removed config keys in crates/ralph/src/migration/registry.rs (and related migration modules if required).
Remove obsolete docs/help references in docs/configuration.md, docs/features/configuration.md, docs/cli.md, and crates/ralph/assets/ralph_readme.md.
Regenerate schema artifacts with make generate and ensure schemas/config.schema.json no longer advertises removed keys.
Run validation with targeted tests plus make macos-ci.
notes: Scope refreshed against current tree: replaced missing crates/ralph/src/cli.rs with crates/ralph/src/cli/run.rs and expanded to include docs/schema/sanity surfaces that currently expose this behavior.

tasks.title: Add .jsonc (JSON with Comments) file format support
tasks.description: Audit all existing files that utilize .json format and add support for .jsonc (JSON with Comments) format. When both .json and .jsonc versions of a file exist, the .json file takes precedence. Files must be checked in precedence order: first for .json, then fallback to .jsonc if .json is not present. This applies to queue files (queue.json/queue.jsonc), done files (done.json/done.jsonc), and config files (config.json/config.jsonc).
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/config.rs` currently hardcodes default queue/done paths to `.ralph/queue.json` and `.ralph/done.json` in `resolve_queue_path` / `resolve_done_path`.
`crates/ralph/src/config.rs` currently resolves project/global config as `config.json` only (`project_config_path`, `global_config_path`) while `load_layer` already parses JSONC.
`crates/ralph/src/config.rs::find_repo_root` only recognizes `.ralph/queue.json` or `.ralph/config.json`, so repos using only `.jsonc` are not discovered via `.ralph` markers.
`crates/ralph/src/queue.rs` already loads queue/done through `jsonc::parse_jsonc`, so parser support exists once file path resolution can locate `.jsonc` files.
`crates/ralph/src/commands/init/writers.rs::write_config` validates existing config with `serde_json::from_str::<Config>`, which rejects comments and conflicts with JSONC support.
`docs/configuration.md` claims JSONC support for config/queue files, so path-resolution behavior must match docs and enforce explicit precedence (`.json` first, `.jsonc` fallback).
plan: Add a shared path-resolution helper for default Ralph files that enforces precedence: prefer `<name>.json` when it exists, otherwise use `<name>.jsonc` when it exists, otherwise return `<name>.json` as the creation default.
Apply the helper in `config.rs` for `resolve_queue_path`, `resolve_done_path`, `project_config_path`, and `global_config_path`; do not alter explicit `queue.file` / `queue.done_file` overrides.
Update `find_repo_root` in `config.rs` to treat `.ralph/queue.jsonc` and `.ralph/config.jsonc` as valid repo markers in addition to `.json` files.
Keep queue/done/config write paths JSON-only by default (no comment round-trip requirement), but ensure existing `.jsonc` files are readable and writable at the resolved path.

Update `commands/init/writers.rs::write_config` existing-file validation to parse via JSONC-capable loader (`config::load_layer` or `jsonc::parse_jsonc`) so commented config files are accepted when not forcing overwrite.
Add regression tests in `crates/ralph/tests/config_test.rs` for `.json` precedence over `.jsonc` and `.jsonc` fallback for queue/done/config resolution and repo-root detection.
Update `docs/configuration.md` to document deterministic filename precedence (`.json` preferred over `.jsonc`) rather than extension-agnostic behavior.
In Phase 2, run the local CI gate (`make macos-ci`) after implementation and fix any failures before completion.
notes: Implementation should centralize precedence logic in one helper to avoid divergent filename checks across functions.
Behavior change is intentional: when both files exist, only `.json` is selected; `.jsonc` is fallback-only.
If additional `.json`/`.jsonc` resolver call-sites are discovered during implementation, extend updates beyond this scope to keep behavior consistent.
Added .jsonc (JSON with Comments) file format support with .json precedence
Core implementation: prefer_json_then_jsonc() helper in config.rs
Updated path resolution for queue, done, and config files
Updated find_repo_root() to detect .jsonc marker files
Updated write_config validation to accept JSONC via load_layer()
Added 12 comprehensive tests covering precedence and fallback
Updated documentation in docs/configuration.md
All 69 config tests pass, full macos-ci gate passes
tasks.request: Audit all existing files that utilize .json format. Add support for .jsonc (JSON comments) format. .json files take precedence over .jsonc files (e.g. config.json takes precedence over config.jsonc if both are present) but both are supported.

custom_fields.affected_areas: config loading, queue loading, repo root detection

tasks.title: Adopt `cargo nextest` in local CI gate to reduce test cycle time
tasks.description:
Opportunity type: performance and developer throughput.

Why it matters:
- Current `make test` performs separate full `cargo test` invocations for all-target and doc tests, increasing repeated work and slowing the mandatory local CI loop.

Proposed approach (minimal slice first):
- Add `cargo-nextest` integration behind `make test` (or `make test-fast`) with equivalent coverage guarantees and clear fallback.

Expansion:
- Standardize test reporting artifacts for faster flaky-test triage and shard-friendly future scaling.

Risks and mitigations:
- Risk: coverage mismatches between legacy and nextest runs.
- Mitigation: parallel trial period comparing pass/fail parity and retaining doc-test coverage explicitly.
evidence: path: Makefile :: `test` target currently runs `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --locked` and `cargo test --workspace --doc --locked` as separate full passes, creating duplicated work.
path: Makefile :: `ci` target depends on `test`, so changing `test` behavior directly affects the mandatory local CI gate without changing dependency order.

path: crates/ralph/tests/makefile_ci_contract_test.rs :: CI contract tests enforce `ci` recipe ordering and macOS gate invariants; migration must preserve these guarantees and may add explicit runner assertions.
path: AGENTS.md and GEMINI.md :: contributor guidance currently describes `make test` as workspace unit + doc tests, which must be updated to reflect nextest + doc-test split.
workflow: rg -n "nextest" Makefile crates docs AGENTS.md GEMINI.md CONTRIBUTING.md :: no existing cargo-nextest integration found in current local CI path.

external: https://nexte.st/docs/running/ :: accessed 2026-02-12 :: canonical nextest invocation is `cargo nextest run`, and doc tests remain unsupported so `cargo test --doc` must stay explicit for parity.
plan: Refactor `Makefile` `test` target to run `cargo nextest run --workspace --all-targets --locked -- --include-ignored` for non-doc tests while preserving existing temp-dir isolation and log-on-failure behavior.
Retain explicit doc-test coverage with `cargo test --workspace --doc --locked -- --include-ignored` so `make test` keeps parity with current coverage expectations.
Implement a clear fallback when `cargo-nextest` is unavailable (automatic legacy `cargo test` fallback or documented opt-in switch) and surface actionable guidance in command output.
Keep `ci`/`macos-ci` dependency order stable, then update Makefile help text plus affected contributor docs to describe the new `make test` execution model.
Update CI contract tests to guard the intended Makefile behavior (including any new `cargo nextest run` expectations) while preserving existing ordering and preflight invariants.
Verify in Phase 2 by running `make test` and `make macos-ci`, confirming pass/fail parity with doc tests retained and measuring reduced wall-clock test time versus legacy flow.
notes: Execution must keep doc-test coverage explicit because cargo-nextest does not currently execute Rust doc tests.
Defaulted Makefile test target to cargo nextest with explicit cargo test fallback and preserved doc-test pass.
Strengthened makefile CI contract test to assert fallback workspace cargo test command remains present.
Updated contributor/docs/template references so make test behavior is consistently documented across AGENTS, GEMINI, CONTRIBUTING, and context assets.
Verification passed: cargo test -p ralph -- test_makefile_test_target_uses_nextest_and_keeps_doc_tests and make macos-ci.

tasks.title: Harden Makefile CI contract tests to enforce full required gate sequence
tasks.description:
Opportunity type: reliability and modernization.

Why it matters:
- CI contract tests check selected ordering and doc sync, but can still miss accidental removal of critical steps from the `ci` recipe if docs are updated to match drift.

Proposed approach (minimal slice first):
- Add explicit invariant tests requiring exact presence/order of the mandatory `ci` dependency chain (`check-env-safety` through `install`).

Expansion:
- Add assertions for `macos-ci` composition and expected non-interactive defaults where appropriate.

Risks and mitigations:
- Risk: over-constraining intentional future CI redesigns.
- Mitigation: keep expected sequence centralized in one test constant and update intentionally with rationale.
evidence: path: Makefile:166 :: `ci` declares the full mandatory dependency chain `check-env-safety check-backup-artifacts deps format type-check lint test build generate install` that should be treated as a strict contract.
path: crates/ralph/tests/makefile_ci_contract_test.rs:260-297 :: current ci invariant only validates relative ordering for `format`, `type-check`, and `lint`, leaving other required steps unguarded.

path: crates/ralph/tests/makefile_ci_contract_test.rs:300-346 :: doc parity currently derives expected pipeline directly from parsed Makefile steps, so docs can drift in lockstep with weakened Makefile ci dependencies.

path: Makefile:227 and crates/ralph/tests/makefile_ci_contract_test.rs:417-455 :: `macos-ci` currently composes `macos-preflight ci macos-build macos-test`, but tests only require `macos-preflight` presence instead of full composition/order.
path: CONTRIBUTING.md:55 and GEMINI.md:10 :: both docs publish the full pipeline string and should be validated against a canonical test constant rather than dynamic Makefile parsing alone.
plan: Introduce canonical constants in `crates/ralph/tests/makefile_ci_contract_test.rs` for the required ordered `ci` chain and `macos-ci` dependencies to centralize contract expectations.
Replace the partial ci-order assertion with strict equality checks between parsed `ci` dependencies and the canonical full required sequence.
Add explicit regression assertions for duplicate required steps in parsed `ci` dependencies so accidental repetition fails loudly.
Strengthen macOS gate coverage by asserting exact `macos-ci` dependency composition/order (`macos-preflight ci macos-build macos-test`).
Update docs parity assertions to compare `CONTRIBUTING.md` and `GEMINI.md` against a pipeline string generated from the canonical required-step constant.

Validate with `cargo test -p ralph makefile_ci_contract_test`, then run perturbation checks during development to ensure missing/reordered/duplicated steps fail as expected before restoring valid state.
notes: Intentional CI redesign is allowed, but must update one canonical constant and related docs-parity assertions in the same change with rationale.
Keep existing parser support for legacy `$(MAKE)`-style ci recipes while enforcing strict equality for the active dependency-list contract.
Hardened Makefile contract parsers to anchor exact  target matching and avoid  false matches.
Added parser resilience for multiline target dependencies and legacy  invocations with flags.
Expanded  with regression tests covering multiline headers and legacy flag handling.
